```
60: export class SccService {
61:   private sccPath: string;
62:   private cache: Map<string, { data: SccMetrics; timestamp: number }> = new Map();
63:   private cacheTTL = 5000; // 5 seconds
64: 
65:   constructor(customPath?: string) {
66:     if (customPath) {
67:       this.sccPath = customPath;
68:     } else {
69:       // Check global config first (for tests)
70:       const globalHome = getMimoHome();
71:       if (globalHome) {
72:         this.sccPath = join(globalHome, "bin", "scc");
73:       } else {
74:         // Fall back to paths module
75:         const paths = getPaths();
76:         this.sccPath = join(paths.mimoDir, "bin", "scc");
77:       }

79:   }

82:     return this.sccPath;

89:   detectPlatform(): SccPlatform {
90:     const platform = process.platform;
91:     const arch = process.arch;
92: 
93:     let os: SccPlatform["os"];
94:     switch (platform) {
95:       case "linux":
96:         os = "linux";
97:         break;
98:       case "darwin":
99:         os = "darwin";
100:         break;
101:       case "win32":
102:         os = "windows";
103:         break;
104:       default:
105:         throw new Error(`Unsupported platform: ${platform}`);
106:     }
107: 
108:     let mappedArch: SccPlatform["arch"];
109:     if (arch === "x64") {
110:       mappedArch = "amd64";
111:     } else if (arch === "arm64") {
112:       mappedArch = "arm64";
113:     } else {
114:       throw new Error(`Unsupported architecture: ${arch}`);
115:     }
116: 
117:     return { os, arch: mappedArch };
118:   }
119: 
120:   getDownloadUrl(platform: SccPlatform): string {
121:     const version = "3.5.0"; // Latest stable version
122:     const ext = platform.os === "windows" ? ".zip" : ".tar.gz";
123:     return `https://github.com/boyter/scc/releases/download/v${version}/scc_${version}_${platform.os}_${platform.arch}${ext}`;
124:   }
125: 
126:   async install(): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
127:     try {
128:       if (this.isInstalled()) {
129:         return { success: true };
130:       }
131: 
132:       const platform = this.detectPlatform();
133:       const url = this.getDownloadUrl(platform);
134:       
135:       // Check global config first (for tests), then fall back to getPaths
136:       const globalHome = getMimoHome();
137:       const binDir = globalHome 
138:         ? join(globalHome, "bin")
139:         : join(getPaths().mimoDir, "bin");
140: 
141:       // Create bin directory if needed
142:       if (!existsSync(binDir)) {
143:         mkdirSync(binDir, { recursive: true });
144:       }
145: 
146:       // Download and extract scc
147:       const ext = platform.os === "windows" ? ".zip" : ".tar.gz";
148:       const downloadPath = join(binDir, `scc${ext}`);
149: 
150:       console.log(`[scc] Downloading from ${url}...`);
151:       
152:       // Download using curl
153:       execSync(`curl -L -o "${downloadPath}" "${url}"`, {
154:         timeout: 120000,
155:       });
156: 
157:       console.log("[scc] Extracting...");
158: 
159:       // Extract
160:       if (platform.os === "windows") {
161:         execSync(`powershell -command "Expand-Archive -Path '${downloadPath}' -DestinationPath '${binDir}' -Force"`);
162:       } else {
163:         execSync(`tar -xzf "${downloadPath}" -C "${binDir}"`);
164:       }
165: 
166:       // Make executable
167:       chmodSync(this.sccPath, 0o755);
168: 
169:       // Clean up archive
170:       execSync(`rm "${downloadPath}"`);
171: 
172:       console.log(`[scc] Installed to ${this.sccPath}`);
173:       return { success: true };
174:     } catch (error) {
175:       console.error("[scc] Installation failed:", error);
176:       return { 
177:         success: false, 
178:         error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error) 
179:       };
180:     }
181:   }
182: 
183:   async runScc(directory: string): Promise<SccMetrics> {
184:     // Check cache first
185:     const cacheKey = directory;
186:     const cached = this.cache.get(cacheKey);
187:     if (cached && Date.now() - cached.timestamp < this.cacheTTL) {
188:       return cached.data;
189:     }
190: 
191:     if (!this.isInstalled()) {
192:       throw new Error("scc is not installed. Run install() first.");
193:     }
194: 
195:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
196:       const args = ["--by-file", "-f", "json", directory];
197:       const child = spawn(this.sccPath, args, {
198:         timeout: 30000, // 30 second timeout
199:       });
200: 
201:       let stdout = "";
202:       let stderr = "";
203: 
204:       child.stdout.on("data", (data) => {
205:         stdout += data.toString();
206:       });
207: 
208:       child.stderr.on("data", (data) => {
209:         stderr += data.toString();
210:       });
211: 
212:       child.on("close", (code) => {
213:         if (code !== 0) {
214:           reject(new Error(`scc exited with code ${code}: ${stderr}`));
215:           return;
216:         }
217: 
218:         try {
219:           const output: SccJsonOutput = JSON.parse(stdout);
220:           const metrics = this.parseSccOutput(output);
221:           
222:           // Cache the result
223:           this.cache.set(cacheKey, { data: metrics, timestamp: Date.now() });
224:           
225:           resolve(metrics);
226:         } catch (error) {
227:           reject(new Error(`Failed to parse scc output: ${error}`));
228:         }
229:       });
230: 
231:       child.on("error", (error) => {
232:         reject(new Error(`Failed to run scc: ${error.message}`));
233:       });
234:     });
235:   }
236: 
237:   private parseSccOutput(output: SccJsonOutput): SccMetrics {
238:     const files = output.files || [];
239:     
240:     // Calculate totals
241:     let totalLines = 0;
242:     let totalCode = 0;
243:     let totalComplexity = 0;
244:     
245:     const languageMap = new Map<string, SccLanguageMetrics>();
246:     const fileMetrics: SccFileMetrics[] = [];
247: 
248:     for (const file of files) {
249:       totalLines += file.lines;
250:       totalCode += file.code;
251:       totalComplexity += file.complexity || 0;
252: 
253:       // Language aggregation
254:       const lang = file.language || "Unknown";
255:       const existing = languageMap.get(lang);
256:       if (existing) {
257:         existing.files++;
258:         existing.lines += file.lines;
259:         existing.code += file.code;
260:         existing.comment += file.comment;
261:         existing.blank += file.blank;
262:         existing.complexity += file.complexity || 0;
263:       } else {
264:         languageMap.set(lang, {
265:           language: lang,
266:           files: 1,
267:           lines: file.lines,
268:           code: file.code,
269:           comment: file.comment,
270:           blank: file.blank,
271:           complexity: file.complexity || 0,
272:         });
273:       }
274: 
275:       fileMetrics.push({
276:         path: file.filename,
277:         language: lang,
278:         lines: file.lines,
279:         code: file.code,
280:         comment: file.comment,
281:         blank: file.blank,
282:         complexity: file.complexity || 0,
283:       });
284:     }
285: 
286:     return {
287:       linesOfCode: {
288:         added: totalCode, // Will be calculated as delta by ImpactCalculator
289:         removed: 0,
290:         net: totalCode,
291:       },
292:       complexity: {
293:         cyclomatic: totalComplexity,
294:         cognitive: 0, // scc doesn't provide cognitive complexity
295:         estimatedMinutes: Math.ceil(totalCode / 10), // Rough estimate: 10 lines per minute
296:       },
297:       byLanguage: Array.from(languageMap.values()),
298:       byFile: fileMetrics,
299:     };
300:   }
301: 
302:   clearCache(directory?: string): void {
303:     if (directory) {
304:       this.cache.delete(directory);
305:     } else {
306:       this.cache.clear();
307:     }
308:   }
309: }
310: 
311: let _sccService: SccService | undefined;
312: 
313: export function getSccService(): SccService {
314:   if (!_sccService) {
315:     _sccService = new SccService();
316:   }
317:   return _sccService;
318: }
319: 
320: export const sccService = getSccService();
```

```
291:   private async calculateFileChanges(
292:     upstreamPath: string,
293:     agentWorkspacePath: string
294:   ): Promise<FileImpact[]> {

300: 
301:   private async scanDirectory(
302:     dirPath: string,
303:     basePath: string,
304:     fileMap: Map<string, { checksum: string; size: number }>
305:   ): Promise<void> {
306:     if (!existsSync(dirPath)) return;
307: 
308:     const entries = readdirSync(dirPath, { withFileTypes: true });
309: 
310:     for (const entry of entries) {
311:       const fullPath = join(dirPath, entry.name);
312:       const relativePath = relative(basePath, fullPath);
313: 
314:       // Skip hidden files and directories
315:       if (entry.name.startsWith(".")) continue;
316: 
317:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
318:         await this.scanDirectory(fullPath, basePath, fileMap);
319:       } else {
320:         const stats = statSync(fullPath);
321:         const content = readFileSync(fullPath);
322:         const checksum = crypto.createHash("md5").update(content).digest("hex");
323:         
324:         fileMap.set(relativePath, { checksum, size: stats.size });
325:       }
326:     }
327:   }
328: 
329:   private calculateTrends(sessionId: string, current: ImpactMetrics): ImpactTrend {
330:     const previous = this.previousStates.get(sessionId);
331:     
332:     if (!previous) {
333:       // No previous state - all trends are stable
334:       return {
335:         files: { new: "→", changed: "→", deleted: "→" },
336:         linesOfCode: { added: "→", removed: "→", net: "→" },
337:         complexity: { cyclomatic: "→", cognitive: "→" },
338:       };
339:     }
340: 
341:     const getTrend = (current: number, previous: number): string => {
342:       if (current > previous) return "↑";
343:       if (current < previous) return "↓";
344:       return "→";
345:     };
346: 
347:     return {
348:       files: {
349:         new: getTrend(current.files.new, previous.fileCounts.new),
350:         changed: getTrend(current.files.changed, previous.fileCounts.changed),
351:         deleted: getTrend(current.files.deleted, previous.fileCounts.deleted),
352:       },
353:       linesOfCode: {
354:         added: getTrend(current.linesOfCode.added, previous.loc.added),
355:         removed: getTrend(current.linesOfCode.removed, previous.loc.removed),
356:         net: getTrend(current.linesOfCode.net, previous.loc.net),
357:       },
358:       complexity: {
359:         cyclomatic: getTrend(current.complexity.cyclomatic, previous.complexity.cyclomatic),
360:         cognitive: getTrend(current.complexity.cognitive, previous.complexity.cognitive),
361:       },
362:     };
363:   }
364: 
365:   clearState(sessionId?: string): void {
366:     if (sessionId) {
367:       this.previousStates.delete(sessionId);
368:     } else {
369:       this.previousStates.clear();
370:     }
371:   }
372: }
```

```
38: export class ImpactRepository {
39:   private getImpactDir(projectId: string): string {
40:     return join(getPaths().projects, projectId, "impacts");
41:   }
42: 
43:   private getImpactPath(projectId: string, sessionId: string, commitHash: string): string {
44:     return join(this.getImpactDir(projectId), `${sessionId}-${commitHash}.yaml`);
45:   }
46: 
47:   ensureImpactDir(projectId: string): void {
48:     const impactDir = this.getImpactDir(projectId);
49:     if (!existsSync(impactDir)) {
50:       mkdirSync(impactDir, { recursive: true });
51:     }
52:   }
53: 
54:   save(record: ImpactRecord): void {
55:     this.ensureImpactDir(record.projectId);
56:     
57:     const filePath = this.getImpactPath(record.projectId, record.sessionId, record.commitHash);
58:     const yamlContent = YAML.stringify({
59:       ...record,
60:       commitDate: record.commitDate.toISOString(),
61:     });
62:     
63:     writeFileSync(filePath, yamlContent, "utf-8");
64:   }
65: 
66:   findByProject(projectId: string): ImpactRecord[] {
67:     const impactDir = this.getImpactDir(projectId);
68:     
69:     if (!existsSync(impactDir)) {
70:       return [];
71:     }
72: 
73:     const files = readdirSync(impactDir).filter(f => f.endsWith(".yaml"));
74:     const records: ImpactRecord[] = [];
75: 
76:     for (const file of files) {
77:       const filePath = join(impactDir, file);
78:       try {
79:         const content = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
80:         const data = YAML.parse(content);
81:         records.push({
82:           ...data,
83:           commitDate: new Date(data.commitDate),
84:         });
85:       } catch (error) {
86:         console.error(`[impact] Failed to load impact record from ${filePath}:`, error);
87:       }
88:     }
89: 
90:     // Sort by commit date descending (newest first)
91:     return records.sort((a, b) => b.commitDate.getTime() - a.commitDate.getTime());
92:   }
93: 
94:   findBySession(projectId: string, sessionId: string): ImpactRecord[] {
95:     return this.findByProject(projectId).filter(r => r.sessionId === sessionId);
96:   }
97: 
98:   findByCommitHash(projectId: string, commitHash: string): ImpactRecord | null {
99:     const records = this.findByProject(projectId);
100:     return records.find(r => r.commitHash === commitHash) || null;
101:   }
102: 
103:   delete(projectId: string, sessionId: string, commitHash: string): void {
104:     const filePath = this.getImpactPath(projectId, sessionId, commitHash);
105:     if (existsSync(filePath)) {
106:       const fs = require("fs");
107:       fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
108:     }
109:   }
110: }
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 22:57:28)

The fix is complete. 

## Summary

**Issue:** `ReferenceError: Cannot access 'sccService' before initialization`

**Root Cause:** The error was a Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ) issue caused by the destructuring pattern in the dynamic import:

```typescript
const { sccService } = await import("../impact/scc-service.js");
```

When destructuring named exports that are initialized at module load time (like `export const sccService = getSccService()`), the JavaScript/TypeScript engine can sometimes hit TDZ errors depending on how the module graph resolves.

**Fix:** Changed the import pattern to access the property on the module object instead of destructuring:

```typescript
const sccServiceModule = await import("../impact/scc-service.js");
// ...later use...
const sccInstalled = sccServiceModule.sccService.isInstalled();
```

This avoids the TDZ issue by deferring the property access until the module is fully loaded.

## Summary

**Issue:** `ReferenceError: Cannot access 'sccService' before initialization`

**Root Cause:** The error was a Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ) issue caused by the destructuring pattern in the dynamic import:

```typescript
const { sccService } = await import("../impact/scc-service.js");
```

When destructuring named exports that are initialized at module load time (like `export const sccService = getSccService()`), the JavaScript/TypeScript engine can sometimes hit TDZ errors depending on how the module graph resolves.

**Fix:** Changed the import pattern to access the property on the module object instead of destructuring:

```typescript
const sccServiceModule = await import("../impact/scc-service.js");
// ...later use...
const sccInstalled = sccServiceModule.sccService.isInstalled();
```

This avoids the TDZ issue by deferring the property access until the module is fully loaded.

```typescript
let _sccService: SccService | undefined;

export function getSccService(): SccService {
  if (!_sccService) {
    _sccService = new SccService();
  }
  return _sccService;
}

export const sccService = getSccService();
```

The issue is that `export const sccService = getSccService();` is called at module load time. If there's a circular dependency or if `SccService` constructor accesses something that hasn't been initialized yet, this could cause a TDZ error.

Looking at the constructor:
```typescript
constructor(customPath?: string) {
  if (customPath) {
    this.sccPath = customPath;
  } else {
    // Check global config first (for tests)
    const globalHome = getMimoHome();
    if (globalHome) {
      this.sccPath = join(globalHome, "bin", "scc");
    } else {
      // Fall back to paths module
      const paths = getPaths();
      this.sccPath = join(paths.mimoDir, "bin", "scc");
    }
  }
}
```

The problem could be in the module initialization order.

The issue is clear now. The `commits/service.ts` has a **static import** of `impactCalculator` from `../impact/calculator.js`. This causes a chain:

1. `commits/service.ts` is loaded
2. It static-imports `impact/calculator.js`
3. `calculator.ts` static-imports type definitions from `scc-service.js` 
4. When the dynamic import happens in sessions/routes, the module is already being loaded due to the static import chain, causing TDZ issues with the `sccService` export

The solution is to change the static import in commits/service.ts to a dynamic import as well, OR to not export the singleton instance as a const but rather as a getter function (which is already available via `getSccService()`).

Looking at the error, the issue is that even with dynamic import, when the module is evaluated, the TDZ for `sccService` is hit because there's likely a circular dependency or load order issue.

The cleanest fix is to use the `getSccService()` function instead of the `sccService` const. This function is designed to avoid TDZ issues by creating the instance lazily.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 22:59:35)

The error is happening because `sccService` is initialized at module load time (line 320), and when the module is imported dynamically while the module graph is already being evaluated (due to static imports in commits/service.ts), it creates a TDZ issue.

The best solution is to not export the singleton instance directly, but instead export a getter function. But to keep changes minimal, let me look at the actual structure and provide a fix.

Actually, looking at the error more carefully:
```
ReferenceError: Cannot access 'sccService' before initialization.
```

This is a classic TDZ error when using `let` or `const` in a temporal dead zone. The issue is that when the dynamic import happens, the module is still being evaluated (because of the circular dependency through commits/service), and `sccService` hasn't been assigned yet.

The safest fix is to change how we access `sccService` - use the getter function `getSccService()` instead of the exported const. Let me check if we can just use the getter.
## Agent (2026-04-06 22:59:38)

```
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { spawn, ChildProcess } from "child_process";
2: import { existsSync } from "fs";
3: import { createConnection } from "net";
4: 
5: const PORT_RANGE_START = 8000;
6: const PORT_RANGE_END = 9000;
7: 
8: interface RunningServer {
9:   port: number;
10:   process: ChildProcess;
11:   repoPath: string;
12: }
13: 
14: export class FossilServerManager {
15:   private portsInUse: Set<number> = new Set();
16:   private runningServers: Map<string, RunningServer> = new Map();
17: 
18:   private async isPortAvailable(port: number): Promise<boolean> {
19:     return new Promise((resolve) => {
20:       const socket = createConnection(port, "localhost");
21:       socket.on("connect", () => {
22:         socket.destroy();
23:         resolve(false); // Port is in use
24:       });
25:       socket.on("error", () => {
26:         resolve(true); // Port is available
27:       });
28:     });
29:   }
30: 
31:   async assignPort(): Promise<number | null> {
32:     for (let port = PORT_RANGE_START; port <= PORT_RANGE_END; port++) {
33:       if (!this.portsInUse.has(port)) {
34:         // Check if port is actually available on the system
35:         const available = await this.isPortAvailable(port);
36:         if (available) {
37:           this.portsInUse.add(port);
38:           return port;
39:         }
40:       }
41:     }
42:     return null;
43:   }
44: 
45:   releasePort(port: number): void {
46:     this.portsInUse.delete(port);
47:   }
48: 
49:   async startServer(sessionId: string, repoPath: string): Promise<{ port: number } | { error: string }> {
50:     // Check if server already running for this session
51:     if (this.runningServers.has(sessionId)) {
52:       const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId)!;
53:       return { port: server.port };
54:     }
55: 
56:     // Check if repo exists
57:     if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {
58:       return { error: `Repository not found: ${repoPath}` };
59:     }
60: 
61:     // Assign port
62:     const port = await this.assignPort();
63:     if (port === null) {
64:       return { error: "PORTS_EXHAUSTED" };
65:     }
66: 
67:     // Start fossil server
68:     const proc = spawn("fossil", ["server", "--port", port.toString(), repoPath], {
69:       stdio: "pipe",
70:       detached: false,
71:     });
72: 
73:     // Wait for server to start (simple check: wait a bit)
74:     await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
75: 
76:     // Check if process started successfully
77:     if (proc.pid === undefined) {
78:       this.releasePort(port);
79:       return { error: "Failed to start Fossil server" };
80:     }
81: 
82:     // Track running server
83:     this.runningServers.set(sessionId, {
84:       port,
85:       process: proc,
86:       repoPath,
87:     });
88: 
89:     // Handle process exit
90:     proc.on("exit", () => {
91:       this.runningServers.delete(sessionId);
92:       this.releasePort(port);
93:     });
94: 
95:     return { port };
96:   }
97: 
98:   async stopServer(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
99:     const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId);
100:     if (!server) {
101:       return;
102:     }
103: 
104:     // Kill the process
105:     server.process.kill("SIGTERM");
106: 
107:     // Wait for process to exit
108:     await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
109:       server.process.on("exit", () => {
110:         resolve();
111:       });
112:       // Force kill after 5 seconds
113:       setTimeout(() => {
114:         server.process.kill("SIGKILL");
115:         resolve();
116:       }, 5000);
117:     });
118: 
119:     // Cleanup
120:     this.runningServers.delete(sessionId);
121:     this.releasePort(server.port);
122:   }
123: 
124:   getRunningServer(sessionId: string): { port: number; repoPath: string } | null {
125:     const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId);
126:     if (!server) {
127:       return null;
128:     }
129:     return { port: server.port, repoPath: server.repoPath };
130:   }
131: 
132:   isServerRunning(sessionId: string): boolean {
133:     return this.runningServers.has(sessionId);
134:   }
135: 
136:   getActiveServerCount(): number {
137:     return this.runningServers.size;
138:   }
139: 
140:   async stopAllServers(): Promise<void> {
141:     const stopPromises = Array.from(this.runningServers.keys()).map((sessionId) =>
142:       this.stopServer(sessionId)
143:     );
144:     await Promise.all(stopPromises);
145:   }
146: }
147: 
148: export const fossilServerManager = new FossilServerManager();
```

```
101: const server = Bun.serve({
102:   async fetch(req: Request, server: any) {
103:     const url = new URL(req.url);
104:     
105:     // Handle WebSocket upgrade requests
106:     if (req.headers.get("upgrade") === "websocket") {
107:       const type = url.pathname.split('/')[2]; // /ws/agent or /ws/chat
108:       
109:       if (type === 'agent') {
110:         const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
111:         if (!token) {
112:           console.log("[WS] Missing token");
113:           return new Response("Missing token", { status: 400 });
114:         }
115:         
116:         const payload = await agentService.verifyAgentToken(token);
117:         if (!payload) {
118:           console.log("[WS] Invalid token");
119:           return new Response("Invalid token", { status: 401 });
120:         }
121:         
122:         console.log("[WS] Token verified, agentId:", payload.agentId);
123:         
124:         const upgraded = server.upgrade(req, {
125:           data: {
126:             connectionType: 'agent',
127:             agentId: payload.agentId,
128:             url: req.url
129:           }
130:         });
131:         
132:         if (!upgraded) {
133:           console.log("[WS] WebSocket upgrade failed");
134:           return new Response("WebSocket upgrade failed", { status: 500 });
135:         }
136:         console.log("[WS] WebSocket upgraded successfully for agent:", payload.agentId);
137:         return undefined;
138:       }
139:       
140:       if (type === 'chat') {
141:         const sessionId = url.pathname.split('/')[3];
142:         if (!sessionId) {
143:           return new Response("Missing sessionId", { status: 400 });
144:         }
145:         
146:         const upgraded = server.upgrade(req, {
147:           data: {
148:             connectionType: 'chat',
149:             sessionId,
150:             url: req.url
151:           }
152:         });
153:         
154:         if (!upgraded) {
155:           return new Response("WebSocket upgrade failed", { status: 500 });
156:         }
157:         return undefined;
158:       }
159:       
160:       return new Response("Unknown WebSocket endpoint", { status: 404 });
161:     }
162:     
163:     return app.fetch(req);
164:   },
165:   port: PORT,
166:   websocket: {
167:     // WebSocket handler for different connection types
168:     async message(ws, message) {
169:       try {
170:         const data = JSON.parse(message as string);
171:         const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
172:         
173:         switch (connectionType) {
174:           case 'agent':
175:             await handleAgentMessage(ws, data);
176:             break;
177:           case 'chat':
178:             await handleChatMessage(ws, data);
179:             break;
180:           default:
181:             console.log("Unknown connection type");
182:         }
183:       } catch (error) {
184:         console.error("WebSocket message error:", error);
185:       }
186:     },
187:     async open(ws) {
188:       const url = new URL(ws.data.url);
189:       const type = url.pathname.split('/')[2]; // /ws/chat or /ws/agent
190:       
191:       if (type === 'chat') {
192:         // Chat connection
193:         const sessionId = url.pathname.split('/')[3];
194:         ws.data.connectionType = 'chat';
195:         ws.data.sessionId = sessionId;
196:         
197:         // Add to session subscribers
198:         if (!chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
199:           chatSessions.set(sessionId, new Set());
200:         }
201:         chatSessions.get(sessionId).add(ws);
202:         
203:         // Send chat history
204:         const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
205:         ws.send(JSON.stringify({
206:           type: 'history',
207:           messages: history,
208:         }));
209:         
210:         console.log(`Chat client connected to session ${sessionId}`);
211:       } else {
212:         // Agent connection
213:         const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
214:         
215:         if (!token) {
216:           ws.close(1008, "Missing token");
217:           return;
218:         }
219: 
220:         const payload = await agentService.verifyAgentToken(token);
221:         if (!payload) {
222:           ws.close(1008, "Invalid token");
223:           return;
224:         }
225: 
226:         ws.data.connectionType = 'agent';
227:         ws.data.agentId = payload.agentId;
228:         ws.data.authenticated = true;  // Mark as authenticated, waiting for agent_ready
229:         
230:         await agentService.handleAgentConnect(payload.agentId, ws);
231:         console.log(`Agent ${payload.agentId} connected, waiting for agent_ready`);
232:       }
233:     },
234:     async close(ws) {
235:       const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
236:       
237:       if (connectionType === 'chat') {
238:         // Remove from session subscribers
239:         const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
240:         if (chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
241:           chatSessions.get(sessionId).delete(ws);
242:           if (chatSessions.get(sessionId).size === 0) {
243:             chatSessions.delete(sessionId);
244:           }
245:         }
246:         console.log(`Chat client disconnected from session ${sessionId}`);
247:       } else if (connectionType === 'agent') {
248:         const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
249:         if (agentId) {
250:           await agentService.handleAgentDisconnect(agentId);
251:           console.log(`Agent ${agentId} disconnected`);
252: 
253:           // Stop Fossil servers for assigned sessions after grace period
254:           const agent = await agentRepository.findById(agentId);
255:           if (agent && agent.sessionIds && agent.sessionIds.length > 0) {
256:             // 30-second grace period for unexpected disconnects
257:             setTimeout(async () => {
258:               // Check if agent reconnected
259:               if (!agentService.isAgentOnline(agentId)) {
260:                 // Agent didn't reconnect, stop all servers
261:                 for (const sessionId of agent.sessionIds) {
262:                   if (fossilServerManager.isServerRunning(sessionId)) {
263:                     await fossilServerManager.stopServer(sessionId);
264:                     console.log(`Stopped Fossil server for session ${sessionId}`);
265:                   }
266:                 }
267:               }
268:             }, 30000);
269:           }
270:         }
271:       }
272:     },
273:   },
274: });
275: 
276: // Handle agent messages
277: async function handleAgentMessage(ws, data) {
278:   console.log("[agent] Received message:", data.type, data);
279:   process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
280:   
281:   switch (data.type) {
282:     case "ping":
283:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "pong" }));
284:       break;
285:     case "agent_ready":
286:       console.log("[agent] Agent ready:", data.agentId, "workdir:", data.workdir);
287:       process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
288:       
289:       // Store workdir for relative path computation
290:       const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
291:       if (data.workdir) {
292:         agentService.handleAgentConnect(agentId, ws, data.workdir);
293:       }
294:       
295:       // Start Fossil servers for sessions assigned to this agent
296:       // Note: Sessions are assigned via assignedAgentId, not in agent.sessionIds
297:       const sessions = await sessionRepository.findByAssignedAgentId(agentId);
298:       console.log("[agent] Found", sessions.length, "sessions assigned to agent", agentId);
299:       process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
300:       
301:       if (sessions.length > 0) {
302:         const sessionsReady = [];
303:         const workdir = agentService.getAgentWorkdir(agentId);
304:         console.log("[agent] Workdir:", workdir);
305:         process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
306:         
307:         for (const session of sessions) {
308:           const sessionId = session.id;
309:           console.log("[agent] Session:", sessionId, "status:", session.status);
310:           process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
311:           
312:           if (session.status === "active") {
313:             const fossilPath = `${session.upstreamPath}/../repo.fossil`;
314:             console.log("[agent] Starting fossil server for session:", sessionId, "fossil:", fossilPath);
315:             process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
316:             
317:             const result = await fossilServerManager.startServer(sessionId, fossilPath);
318:             console.log("[agent] Fossil server result:", result);
319:             process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
320:             
321:             if ('port' in result) {
322:               // Update session with port
323:               await sessionRepository.update(sessionId, { port: result.port });
324:               
325:               // Get session with credentials
326:               const sessionWithCreds = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
327:               
328:               sessionsReady.push({
329:                 sessionId,
330:                 port: result.port,
331:                 agentWorkspaceUser: sessionWithCreds?.agentWorkspaceUser,
332:                 agentWorkspacePassword: sessionWithCreds?.agentWorkspacePassword,
333:               });
334:             } else {
335:               console.error("[agent] Failed to start fossil server:", result.error);
336:             }
337:           }
338:         }
339:         
340:         // Send session_ready message to agent
341:         if (sessionsReady.length > 0) {
342:           const message = {
343:             type: 'session_ready',
344:             platformUrl: `http://localhost:${PORT}`,
345:             sessions: sessionsReady,
346:           };
347:           console.log("[agent] Sending session_ready:", JSON.stringify(message));
348:           process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
349:           ws.send(JSON.stringify(message));
350:         } else {
351:           console.log("[agent] No sessions ready to send");
352:         }
353:       } else {
354:         console.log("[agent] No sessions assigned to agent");
355:       }
356:       break;
357:     case "thought_start":
358:       {
359:         const startSessionId = data.sessionId;
360:         if (!startSessionId) {
361:           console.log("No sessionId in thought_start");
362:           return;
363:         }
364:         
365:         // Start new thought buffer
366:         thoughtBuffers.set(startSessionId, "");
367:         
368:         // Forward to clients
369:         const subscribers = chatSessions.get(startSessionId);
370:         if (subscribers) {
371:           subscribers.forEach(client => {
372:             if (client.readyState === 1) {
373:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
374:                 type: data.type,
375:                 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
376:               }));
377:             }
378:           });
379:         }
380:       }
381:       break;
382:       
383:     case "thought_chunk":
384:       {
385:         const chunkSessionId = data.sessionId;
386:         if (!chunkSessionId) {
387:           console.log("No sessionId in thought_chunk");
388:           return;
389:         }
390:         
391:         // Accumulate thought chunks
392:         const currentThoughtBuffer = thoughtBuffers.get(chunkSessionId) || "";

645: // Handle chat messages
646: async function handleChatMessage(ws, data) {
647:   const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
648:   
649:   switch (data.type) {
650:     case "send_message":
651:       // Save user message
652:       await chatService.saveMessage(sessionId, {
653:         role: 'user',
654:         content: data.content,
655:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
656:       });
657: 
658:       // Broadcast to all clients in session
659:       const subscribers = chatSessions.get(sessionId);
660:       if (subscribers) {
661:         subscribers.forEach(client => {
662:           if (client.readyState === 1) {
663:             client.send(JSON.stringify({
664:               type: 'message',
665:               role: 'user',
666:               content: data.content,
667:               timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
668:             }));
669:           }
670:         });
671:       }
672: 
673:       // Get session to find assigned agent
674:       const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
675:       if (session?.assignedAgentId) {
676:         // Forward to agent if connected
677:         const ws = agentService.getAgentConnection(session.assignedAgentId);
678:         if (ws && ws.readyState === 1) {
679:           ws.send(JSON.stringify({
680:             type: 'user_message',
681:             sessionId: sessionId,
682:             content: data.content,
683:           }));
684:         }
685:       }
686:       break;
687: 
688:     case "set_model":
689:       // Forward model change to agent
690:       const modelSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
691:       if (modelSession?.assignedAgentId) {
692:         const agentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(modelSession.assignedAgentId);
693:         if (agentWs && agentWs.readyState === 1) {
694:           agentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
695:             type: 'set_model',
696:             sessionId: sessionId,
697:             modelId: data.modelId,
698:           }));
699:         }
700:       }
701:       break;
702: 
703:     case "set_mode":
704:       // Forward mode change to agent
705:       const modeSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
706:       if (modeSession?.assignedAgentId) {
707:         const modeAgentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(modeSession.assignedAgentId);
708:         if (modeAgentWs && modeAgentWs.readyState === 1) {
709:           modeAgentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
710:             type: 'set_mode',
711:             sessionId: sessionId,
712:             modeId: data.modeId,
713:           }));
714:         }
715:       }
716:       break;
717: 
718:     case "request_state":
719:       // Forward state request to agent
720:       const stateSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
721:       if (stateSession?.assignedAgentId) {
722:         const stateAgentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(stateSession.assignedAgentId);
723:         if (stateAgentWs && stateAgentWs.readyState === 1) {
724:           stateAgentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
725:             type: 'request_state',
726:             sessionId: sessionId,
727:           }));
728:         }
729:       }
730:       break;
731:       
732:     case "request_replay":
733:       const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
734:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({
735:         type: 'history',
736:         messages: history,
737:       }));
738:       break;
739:       
740:     default:
741:       console.log("Unknown chat message type:", data.type);
742:   }
743: }
744:
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:03:39)

Done! I've added process exit handlers to `vcs/fossil-server.ts` to ensure fossil servers are properly cleaned up when the application exits:

1. **`process.on("exit")`** - Called when the process exits normally (synchronous)
2. **`process.on("SIGINT")`** - Called on Ctrl+C (asynchronous)
3. **`process.on("SIGTERM")`** - Called on `kill` command (asynchronous)

The handlers call `fossilServerManager.stopAllServers()` which:
- Gracefully stops each server with SIGTERM
- Waits up to 5 seconds for each to exit
- Force kills with SIGKILL after timeout if still running
- Cleans up port and state tracking

This mirrors the pattern already used in `vcs/server.ts`.
## Agent (2026-04-06 23:03:49)

Done! I've added process exit handlers to ensure fossil servers are properly cleaned up when the application exits:

**Added to `vcs/fossil-server.ts`:**

1. **`process.on("exit")`** - Called when the process exits normally
2. **`process.on("SIGINT")`** - Called on Ctrl+C 
3. **`process.on("SIGTERM")`** - Called on `kill` command

Each handler calls `fossilServerManager.stopAllServers()` which:
- Gracefully stops each server with SIGTERM
- Waits up to 5 seconds for each to exit
- Force kills with SIGKILL after timeout if still running
- Cleans up port and state tracking

This mirrors the cleanup pattern already used in `vcs/server.ts`.

1. **Fixed TDZ error in `sessions/routes.tsx`**: Changed from destructuring `const { sccService }` to using the getter function `const { getSccService }` to avoid Temporal Dead Zone issues with module initialization.

2. **Fixed path bug in `impact/scc-service.ts`**: Changed `paths.mimoDir` to `paths.root` (2 occurrences) since `getPaths()` returns `root`, not `mimoDir`.

3. **Added fossil server cleanup in `vcs/fossil-server.ts`**: Added `process.on("exit")`, `process.on("SIGINT")`, and `process.on("SIGTERM")` handlers to properly stop all fossil servers when the application exits.

I can see the issue now! The agent is pushing to `http://agent-e36e608c@localhost:8001/` - note the port is **8001**. But from the code I see:

3. The agent receives `platformUrl: http://localhost:${PORT}` which is the **platform port** (3000), not the fossil server port

```
181:   private async handleSessionReady(message: any): Promise<void> {
182:     const { platformUrl, sessions } = message;
183: 
184:     if (!sessions || sessions.length === 0) {
185:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessions assigned");
186:       return;
187:     }

197:         const platformHost = platformUrl
198:           .replace(/^https?:\/\//, "")
199:           .replace(/\/+$/, "");

219:         console.error(
220:           `[mimo-agent] Failed to setup session ${sessionId}:`,

222:         );
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:07:40)

I can see the issue now. Looking at `setupCheckout`:

1. The agent receives `session_ready` message with session info (port, credentials)
2. It calls `setupCheckout` which:
   - Creates/open the fossil repo at the first call
   - Sets the remote URL with credentials
3. BUT if the agent reconnects and gets a NEW port (because the server restarted), it doesn't update the remote URL!

The problem is:
1. `setupCheckout` checks if `repoPath` exists and if so, it just opens it without updating the remote URL
2. The fossil remote URL is only set during initial clone (`fossil remote-url ${cloneUrl}`)
3. When the port changes (server restart), the remote URL still points to the old port

Let me verify this is the issue and see how we can fix it:
## Agent (2026-04-06 23:07:44)

I can see the issue now. Looking at the agent code in `setupCheckout`:

**Problem:** When the fossil server restarts with a new port, the agent's remote URL still points to the old port. The `setupCheckout` function only sets the remote URL during initial clone - it doesn't update it when the repo already exists.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:07:52)

The bug is clear now. In the `mimo-agent`, the `setupCheckout` function handles three cases:

1. `existsSync(repoPath)` - Fossil repo file exists (line 248-257)
2. `existsSync(join(checkoutPath, ".fossil"))` - Checkout directory exists with .fossil file (line 258-264)
3. `else` - Neither exists, so clone fresh (line 265-301)

The problem is in cases 1 and 2 - when the repository/checkout already exists, it just opens it BUT doesn't update the remote URL. So if the fossil server restarted on a different port, the remote URL still points to the old port.

The fix needs to update the remote URL in cases 1 and 2, similar to how it's done in case 3 (the else branch).

Let me also check what message type is sent when a session's port changes. I need to understand if the agent receives a new `session_ready` message when the port changes.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:08:30)

I found the issue. Looking at the code flow:

1. When `handleSessionReady` is called with existing sessions, it calls `setupCheckout` with the new `fossilUrl` (which includes the new port)
2. `setupCheckout` checks if the repo already exists and if so, only opens it but does NOT update the remote URL
3. Then `sessionManager.createSession` is called which updates the session info in memory

The bug is in `setupCheckout` - when the repo already exists, it should update the remote URL to point to the new port. Let me check what `sessionManager.createSession` does:

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:10:05)

Done! The fix has been committed and pushed.

## Summary

**Root Cause:** When the fossil server restarted (e.g., after platform restart), it would get a new port. The agent's `setupCheckout()` function would:
1. Detect the existing repo
2. Open it
3. BUT NOT update the fossil remote URL to point to the new port
4. `fossil push` would fail with "login failed" because it was trying to push to the old port

**Fix:** Added remote URL update logic to both branches of `setupCheckout()`:
- When `repoPath` exists (`.fossil` file exists)
- When `.fossil` directory exists (checkout is open)

Both branches now update the remote URL with the new port and credentials when a `session_ready` message is received.
## Agent (2026-04-06 23:10:28)

Done! The fix has been committed and pushed.

**Summary of the bug:**

**Root Cause:** When the fossil server restarted (e.g., after platform restart), it would get a new port. The agent's `setupCheckout()` function would:
1. Detect the existing repo
2. Open it
3. **BUT NOT update the fossil remote URL to point to the new port**
4. `fossil push` would fail with "login failed" because it was trying to push to the old port (8000 instead of 8001)

**Fix:** Added remote URL update logic to both branches of `setupCheckout()` in `packages/mimo-agent/src/index.ts`:
- When `repoPath` exists (`.fossil` file exists)  
- When `.fossil` directory exists (checkout is open)

Both branches now update the remote URL with the new port and credentials when a `session_ready` message is received.

## Agent (2026-04-06 23:14:25)

The issue is that fossil is prompting for the password even though we set the remote URL with credentials. This happens because fossil doesn't automatically use credentials from the remote URL for authentication - it stores them separately.

The agent needs to ensure the password is properly set. Let me add logic to set the local password after updating the remote URL:

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:15:05)

Done! The agent now:
1. Updates the remote URL with the new port and credentials
2. Also updates the local fossil user password to match the server password

This should prevent the password prompt when fossil tries to authenticate after the remote URL is changed.
## Agent (2026-04-06 23:15:06)

Done! Committed and pushed.

**Fix:** Added `fossil user password` command after updating the remote URL in both branches of `setupCheckout()`.

The issue was that fossil stores credentials separately from the remote URL. When the URL changes, fossil still needs the local password to match the server password for authentication. Now the agent:

1. Updates the remote URL with new port/credentials
2. Runs `fossil user password <user> <password>` to sync the local password with the server

still is asking for the password whin fossil push or sync

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:15:57)

Let me investigate why fossil is still asking for the password. The issue might be that fossil is using the global config-db to store credentials, or there's a timing issue with when the password is being set.

Let me check what fossil commands are actually available for setting credentials and how they work.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:16:15)

The problem is clearer now. Looking at the fossil.db file, there are entries with the hostname "localhost" but the credentials may not be properly associated. The fossil global config uses a special format for storing credentials by hostname.

When fossil encounters a URL like `http://agent-e36e608c@localhost:8000/`, it looks up the credentials in the global config db based on the hostname "localhost", not based on the full URL or the username in the URL.

Looking at the fossil config entries, I can see entries like:
```
ckout:/tmp/demo/e36e608c-5f61-40fd-866d-499b9f5f7b89//tmp/demo/e36e608c-5f61-40fd-866d-499b9f5f7b89.fossil
```

This is a checkout entry, not a credential entry. The credential entries should look like:
```
http://localhost:8000/
```

with the password stored in the global_config table.

The issue is that when the remote URL is changed from `http://localhost:8000/` to `http://localhost:8001/`, fossil needs the credentials for the new hostname:port combination. Since the agent ran `fossil user password` only locally, it didn't update the global config with the new port credentials.

Let me check the fossil-server.ts to see how it sets up users:

```
<content>1: import { spawn, ChildProcess } from "child_process";
2: import { existsSync } from "fs";
3: import { createConnection } from "net";
4: 
5: const PORT_RANGE_START = 8000;
6: const PORT_RANGE_END = 9000;
7: 
8: interface RunningServer {
9:   port: number;
10:   process: ChildProcess;
11:   repoPath: string;
12: }
13: 
14: export class FossilServerManager {
15:   private portsInUse: Set<number> = new Set();
16:   private runningServers: Map<string, RunningServer> = new Map();
17: 
18:   private async isPortAvailable(port: number): Promise<boolean> {
19:     return new Promise((resolve) => {
20:       const socket = createConnection(port, "localhost");
21:       socket.on("connect", () => {
22:         socket.destroy();
23:         resolve(false); // Port is in use
24:       });
25:       socket.on("error", () => {
26:         resolve(true); // Port is available
27:       });
28:     });
29:   }
30: 
31:   async assignPort(): Promise<number | null> {
32:     for (let port = PORT_RANGE_START; port <= PORT_RANGE_END; port++) {
33:       if (!this.portsInUse.has(port)) {
34:         // Check if port is actually available on the system
35:         const available = await this.isPortAvailable(port);
36:         if (available) {
37:           this.portsInUse.add(port);
38:           return port;
39:         }
40:       }
41:     }
42:     return null;
43:   }
44: 
45:   releasePort(port: number): void {
46:     this.portsInUse.delete(port);
47:   }
48: 
49:   async startServer(sessionId: string, repoPath: string): Promise<{ port: number } | { error: string }> {
50:     // Check if server already running for this session
51:     if (this.runningServers.has(sessionId)) {
52:       const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId)!;
53:       return { port: server.port };
54:     }
55: 
56:     // Check if repo exists
57:     if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {
58:       return { error: `Repository not found: ${repoPath}` };
59:     }
60: 
61:     // Assign port
62:     const port = await this.assignPort();
63:     if (port === null) {
64:       return { error: "PORTS_EXHAUSTED" };
65:     }
66: 
67:     // Start fossil server
68:     const proc = spawn("fossil", ["server", "--port", port.toString(), repoPath], {
69:       stdio: "pipe",
70:       detached: false,
71:     });
72: 
73:     // Wait for server to start (simple check: wait a bit)
74:     await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
75: 
76:     // Check if process started successfully
77:     if (proc.pid === undefined) {
78:       this.releasePort(port);
79:       return { error: "Failed to start Fossil server" };
80:     }
81: 
82:     // Track running server
83:     this.runningServers.set(sessionId, {
84:       port,
85:       process: proc,
86:       repoPath,
87:     });
88: 
89:     // Handle process exit
90:     proc.on("exit", () => {
91:       this.runningServers.delete(sessionId);
92:       this.releasePort(port);
93:     });
94: 
95:     return { port };
96:   }
97:

99:     const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId);
100:     if (!server) {
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:16:38)

The user is saying that fossil is still asking for the password when `fossil push` or `fossil sync` is executed. The `fossil user password` command sets the password for the local user in the repository, but fossil may still prompt for credentials when connecting to the remote server if the credentials aren't properly stored in the global config.

Let me look at how the credentials are set up. The issue might be that:

1. The remote URL is set with credentials: `http://agent-e36e608c:password@localhost:8001/`
2. But fossil doesn't automatically use credentials from the URL for authentication
3. Instead, fossil stores credentials separately in the `~/.fossil` file or requires them to be provided

Let me check the test file to see how credentials are supposed to work:

```
<content>1: import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from "bun:test";
2: import { tmpdir } from "os";
3: import { join } from "path";
4: import { rmSync, existsSync, mkdirSync } from "fs";
5: import { execSync } from "child_process";
6: 
7: describe("Fossil Credential Provisioning Integration Tests", () => {
8:   let testHome: string;
9:   let VCS: any;
10:   let vcs: any;
11:   let fossilServerManager: any;
12: 
13:   beforeEach(async () => {
14:     testHome = join(tmpdir(), `mimo-credential-test-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`);
15:     process.env.MIMO_HOME = testHome;
16:     
17:     try {
18:       rmSync(testHome, { recursive: true, force: true });
19:     } catch {}
20:     
21:     mkdirSync(testHome, { recursive: true });
22: 
23:     const vcsModule = await import("../src/vcs/index.ts");
24:     VCS = vcsModule.VCS;
25:     vcs = new VCS();
26: 
27:     const serverModule = await import("../src/vcs/fossil-server.ts");
28:     fossilServerManager = serverModule.fossilServerManager;
29:   });
30: 
31:   afterEach(async () => {
32:     try {
33:       await fossilServerManager.stopAllServers();
34:     } catch {}
35:     
36:     try {
37:       rmSync(testHome, { recursive: true, force: true });
38:     } catch {}
39:   });
40: 
41:   describe("createFossilUser", () => {
42:     it("should create a fossil user with DEV capabilities", async () => {
43:       const repoPath = join(testHome, "test-user.fossil");
44:       await vcs.createFossilRepo(repoPath);
45: 
46:       const result = await vcs.createFossilUser(repoPath, "test-agent", "testpass123");
47:       
48:       expect(result.success).toBe(true);
49: 
50:       // Verify user exists
51:       const usersOutput = execSync(`fossil user list -R ${repoPath}`).toString();
52:       expect(usersOutput).toContain("test-agent");
53: 
54:       // Verify capabilities (d=develop, i=check-in, o=check-out)
55:       const capsOutput = execSync(`fossil user capabilities test-agent -R ${repoPath}`).toString();
56:       expect(capsOutput).toContain("dio");
57:     });
58: 
59:     it("should fail for non-existent repository", async () => {
60:       const result = await vcs.createFossilUser(
61:         "/nonexistent/repo.fossil",
62:         "test-agent",
63:         "testpass123"
64:       );
65:       
66:       expect(result.success).toBe(false);
67:     });
68:   });
69: 
70:   describe("Agent Clone with Credentials", () => {
71:     it("should allow cloning with authenticated URL", async () => {
72:       const repoPath = join(testHome, "server.fossil");
73:       await vcs.createFossilRepo(repoPath);
74: 
75:       // Create agent user
76:       await vcs.createFossilUser(repoPath, "agent-test", "agentpass123");
77: 
78:       // Start fossil server
79:       const serverResult = await fossilServerManager.startServer("test-session", repoPath);
80:       expect(serverResult.port).toBeDefined();
81:       const port = serverResult.port;
82: 
83:       // Clone with credentials
84:       const checkoutDir = join(testHome, "checkout");
85:       mkdirSync(checkoutDir, { recursive: true });
86: 
87:       const cloneUrl = `http://agent-test:agentpass123@localhost:${port}/`;
88:       execSync(`fossil clone ${cloneUrl} ${join(checkoutDir, "repo.fossil")}`, { cwd: checkoutDir });
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:16:47)

Looking at the test on line 97-106, I see the pattern that works:

1. Clone with credentials: `fossil clone http://user:pass@localhost:port/ repo.fossil`
2. Open checkout: `fossil open --nosync repo.fossil`
3. Set remote URL with credentials: `fossil remote-url http://user:pass@localhost:port/`
4. Set local password: `fossil user password user pass`
5. Add named remote: `fossil remote add server http://user:pass@localhost:port/`
6. Sync: `fossil sync server`

The key insight from line 165-171 is that when you set the remote URL WITHOUT credentials, it will prompt for password. The agent code should be setting the remote URL WITH credentials.

The user is saying "when the remote-url is changed", implying they manually changed it. But the agent code should have automatically updated it when it received the session_ready message with the new port.

So the question is: why didn't the agent update the remote-url? Either:
1. The agent didn't receive the session_ready message
2. The agent received it but didn't process it correctly
3. The remote-url update failed but was silently ignored

The agent logs would show if the session_ready message was received and processed. Let me suggest the user check the agent logs.

Actually, I see that the user has two fossil servers running - one on port 8000 and one on port 8001. This suggests that the platform started a new server (8001) but the agent is still trying to connect to the old one (8000).

The agent code checks if the session already exists in sessionManager and if so, it doesn't call setupCheckout again. Let me check the sessionManager logic.

In index.tsx lines 205-211, the agent calls `this.sessionManager.createSession`. Let me check what createSession does if the session already exists.

In session.ts lines 31-65, createSession creates a new SessionInfo and sets it in the sessions map. If the session already exists, it will be overwritten. So the session should be updated with the new fossilUrl.

But wait, the sessionManager.createSession is called AFTER setupCheckout. And setupCheckout is what updates the remote-url. So the order is:
1. setupCheckout updates remote-url and sets password
2. createSession updates session info

This should work. But if createSession throws an error or fails, the session might not be updated.

Actually, I think the issue might be in the condition. In setupCheckout, the remote-url is only updated if `agentWorkspaceUser && agentWorkspacePassword`. If these are undefined or empty, the update won't happen.

Let me check what the sessionManager.createSession does. It sets the session info including the fossilUrl. But it doesn't call any fossil commands to update the remote-url. The remote-url update happens in setupCheckout.

So if the credentials are missing in the session_ready message, the remote-url won't be updated. Let me suggest checking if the credentials are being sent correctly.

## Agent (2026-04-06 23:19:15)

Good! The agent has been rebuilt with the fix. Now you need to **restart the agent** with the new binary for the changes to take effect.

The agent was running with the old code. To apply the fix:

```bash
# Kill the current agent
pkill -f mimo-agent

### Restart with the new binary
./dist/mimo-agent --token <token> --platform ws://localhost:3000/ws/agent --workdir /tmp/demo
```

When the agent reconnects, the platform will send a new `session_ready` message with the updated port (8001), and the agent will now properly:
1. Update the remote URL to the new port
2. Set the local fossil password to match the server

This should fix the password prompt issue.

Let me check if the agent actually ran the password update command. Let me look at the agent's setupCheckout function to understand what it does.

Actually, the issue might be that fossil doesn't automatically use credentials from the remote URL for authentication. When you set `fossil remote-url http://user:pass@host/`, fossil strips the password and stores only the URL. The password needs to be provided separately.

Looking at the fossil credentials test (lines 97-106), after setting the remote URL with credentials, they also run:
- `fossil user password agent-test agentpass123` (to set local password)


- `fossil remote add server ${cloneUrl}` (to add a named remote with credentials)

The test reveals a critical authentication step: simply setting the remote URL isn't enough. I need to explicitly set the local user password to match the server's password. The `fossil remote add server` command helps by creating a named remote that preserves the credentials, ensuring smooth authentication during push and sync operations.

The `fossil user password` command might actually be setting the password for the server's user, not just the local checkout. This could be why the previous attempt didn't resolve the authentication issue. I'll need to verify how this command interacts with the server-side user configuration.

My next step is to investigate the password update command's behavior by examining the agent logs and tracing exactly what fossil commands are being executed during the agent's setup process.


Once the new agent reconnects:
1. The platform will send `session_ready` with the current port
2. The agent will update the remote URL
3. `fossil user password` will be executed to sync the local password
4. `fossil push` should work without prompting for password

```
240:     sessionId: string,
241:     checkoutPath: string,

245:   ): Promise<void> {
246:     const repoPath = join(checkoutPath, "..", `${sessionId}.fossil`);
247: 
248:     if (existsSync(repoPath)) {
249:       console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Fossil repo exists, opening`);
250:       if (!existsSync(checkoutPath)) {
251:         mkdirSync(checkoutPath, { recursive: true });
252:       }
253:       try {
254:         execSync(`fossil open ${repoPath}`, { cwd: checkoutPath, stdio: "pipe" });
255:       } catch {
256:         // Already open or error, continue
257:       }
258:       // Update remote URL to new port/credentials
259:       if (agentWorkspaceUser && agentWorkspacePassword) {
260:         const url = new URL(fossilUrl);
261:         url.username = agentWorkspaceUser;
262:         url.password = agentWorkspacePassword;
263:         const remoteUrl = url.toString();
264:         console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Updating remote URL to: ${url.protocol}//${url.username}:****@${url.host}/`);
265:         try {
266:           execSync(`fossil remote-url ${remoteUrl}`, { cwd: checkoutPath, stdio: "pipe" });
267:         } catch {
268:           // Ignore error, may already be correct
269:         }
270:         // Ensure local password matches server password
271:         try {
272:           execSync(
273:             `fossil user password ${agentWorkspaceUser} ${agentWorkspacePassword}`,
274:             { cwd: checkoutPath, stdio: "pipe" }
275:           );
276:           console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Updated local user password`);
277:         } catch {
278:           // Ignore error
279:         }
280:       }
281:     } else if (existsSync(join(checkoutPath, ".fossil"))) {
282:       console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Checkout exists, ensuring open`);
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:27:46)

Looking at the code, I see the issue! When an existing checkout is found, it only:
1. Opens the repo
2. Updates the remote URL
3. Sets the local password

But it's **NOT** doing the complete setup like when a new repo is created:
- Adding a named remote "server" with credentials
- Doing an initial sync

Looking at lines 336-347, when cloning, it does:
```typescript
// Add a named remote "server" with credentials embedded
execSync(
  `fossil remote add server ${cloneUrl}`,
  { cwd: checkoutPath, stdio: "pipe" }
);
// Do an initial sync using the named remote with credentials
execSync(
  `fossil sync server`,
  { cwd: checkoutPath, stdio: "pipe" }
);
```

This is missing for the existing checkout paths! The agent needs to:
1. Update the remote URL with credentials
2. Set the local password
3. Add/update the named remote "server" with credentials
4. Do a sync

Let me update the code to include the full setup steps for existing checkouts:

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:28:50)

The agent has been rebuilt. Now the user needs to restart the agent with the new binary. The fix ensures that for existing repos and checkouts, it:

1. Updates the remote URL
2. Sets the local password
3. Removes any existing "server" remote
4. Adds a new "server" remote with credentials
5. Does a sync to verify credentials work

This matches the behavior when cloning a new repo, so it should fix the password prompt issue.
## Agent (2026-04-06 23:28:51)

Done! I've updated the agent to apply the same fossil instructions when receiving `session_ready` for existing checkouts:

**Changes for existing checkouts:**
1. Update remote URL with new port/credentials
2. Set local password
3. **Remove existing "server" remote** (if exists)
4. **Add new "server" remote** with credentials embedded
5. **Do a sync** to verify credentials work

This matches exactly what happens when cloning a new repo. Now when the agent reconnects and receives `session_ready`, it will:
- Remove old remote: `fossil remote rm server`
- Add new remote: `fossil remote add server http://user:pass@host:port/`
- Verify: `fossil sync server`

**You need to restart the agent** with the new binary for this to take effect.
