```
101: const server = Bun.serve({
102:   async fetch(req: Request, server: any) {
103:     const url = new URL(req.url);
104:     
105:     // Handle WebSocket upgrade requests
106:     if (req.headers.get("upgrade") === "websocket") {
107:       const type = url.pathname.split('/')[2]; // /ws/agent or /ws/chat
108:       
109:       if (type === 'agent') {
110:         const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
111:         if (!token) {
112:           console.log("[WS] Missing token");
113:           return new Response("Missing token", { status: 400 });
114:         }
115:         
116:         const payload = await agentService.verifyAgentToken(token);
117:         if (!payload) {
118:           console.log("[WS] Invalid token");
119:           return new Response("Invalid token", { status: 401 });
120:         }
121:         
122:         console.log("[WS] Token verified, agentId:", payload.agentId);
123:         
124:         const upgraded = server.upgrade(req, {
125:           data: {
126:             connectionType: 'agent',
127:             agentId: payload.agentId,
128:             url: req.url
129:           }
130:         });
131:         
132:         if (!upgraded) {
133:           console.log("[WS] WebSocket upgrade failed");
134:           return new Response("WebSocket upgrade failed", { status: 500 });
135:         }
136:         console.log("[WS] WebSocket upgraded successfully for agent:", payload.agentId);
137:         return undefined;
138:       }
139:       
140:       if (type === 'chat') {
141:         const sessionId = url.pathname.split('/')[3];
142:         if (!sessionId) {
143:           return new Response("Missing sessionId", { status: 400 });
144:         }
145:         
146:         const upgraded = server.upgrade(req, {
147:           data: {
148:             connectionType: 'chat',
149:             sessionId,
150:             url: req.url
151:           }
152:         });
153:         
154:         if (!upgraded) {
155:           return new Response("WebSocket upgrade failed", { status: 500 });
156:         }
157:         return undefined;
158:       }
159:       
160:       return new Response("Unknown WebSocket endpoint", { status: 404 });
161:     }
162:     
163:     return app.fetch(req);
164:   },
165:   port: PORT,
166:   websocket: {
167:     // WebSocket handler for different connection types
168:     async message(ws, message) {
169:       try {
170:         const data = JSON.parse(message as string);
171:         const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
172:         
173:         switch (connectionType) {
174:           case 'agent':
175:             await handleAgentMessage(ws, data);
176:             break;
177:           case 'chat':
178:             await handleChatMessage(ws, data);
179:             break;
180:           default:
181:             console.log("Unknown connection type");
182:         }
183:       } catch (error) {
184:         console.error("WebSocket message error:", error);
185:       }
186:     },
187:     async open(ws) {
188:       const url = new URL(ws.data.url);
189:       const type = url.pathname.split('/')[2]; // /ws/chat or /ws/agent
190:       
191:       if (type === 'chat') {
192:         // Chat connection
193:         const sessionId = url.pathname.split('/')[3];
194:         ws.data.connectionType = 'chat';
195:         ws.data.sessionId = sessionId;
196:         
197:         // Add to session subscribers
198:         if (!chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
199:           chatSessions.set(sessionId, new Set());
200:         }
201:         chatSessions.get(sessionId).add(ws);
202:         
203:         // Send chat history
204:         const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
205:         ws.send(JSON.stringify({
206:           type: 'history',
207:           messages: history,
208:         }));
209:         
210:         console.log(`Chat client connected to session ${sessionId}`);
211:       } else {
212:         // Agent connection
213:         const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
214:         
215:         if (!token) {
216:           ws.close(1008, "Missing token");
217:           return;
218:         }
219: 
220:         const payload = await agentService.verifyAgentToken(token);
221:         if (!payload) {
222:           ws.close(1008, "Invalid token");
223:           return;
224:         }
225: 
226:         ws.data.connectionType = 'agent';
227:         ws.data.agentId = payload.agentId;
228:         ws.data.authenticated = true;  // Mark as authenticated, waiting for agent_ready
229:         
230:         await agentService.handleAgentConnect(payload.agentId, ws);
231:         console.log(`Agent ${payload.agentId} connected, waiting for agent_ready`);
232:       }
233:     },
234:     async close(ws) {
235:       const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
236:       
237:       if (connectionType === 'chat') {
238:         // Remove from session subscribers
239:         const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
240:         if (chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
241:           chatSessions.get(sessionId).delete(ws);
242:           if (chatSessions.get(sessionId).size === 0) {
243:             chatSessions.delete(sessionId);
244:           }
245:         }
246:         console.log(`Chat client disconnected from session ${sessionId}`);
247:       } else if (connectionType === 'agent') {
248:         const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
249:         if (agentId) {
250:           await agentService.handleAgentDisconnect(agentId);
251:           console.log(`Agent ${agentId} disconnected`);
252: 
253:           // Stop Fossil servers for assigned sessions after grace period
254:           const agent = await agentRepository.findById(agentId);
255:           if (agent && agent.sessionIds && agent.sessionIds.length > 0) {
256:             // 30-second grace period for unexpected disconnects
257:             setTimeout(async () => {
258:               // Check if agent reconnected
259:               if (!agentService.isAgentOnline(agentId)) {
260:                 // Agent didn't reconnect, stop all servers
261:                 for (const sessionId of agent.sessionIds) {
262:                   if (fossilServerManager.isServerRunning(sessionId)) {
263:                     await fossilServerManager.stopServer(sessionId);
264:                     console.log(`Stopped Fossil server for session ${sessionId}`);
265:                   }
266:                 }
267:               }
268:             }, 30000);
269:           }
270:         }
271:       }
272:     },
273:   },
274: });
275: 
276: // Handle agent messages
277: async function handleAgentMessage(ws, data) {
278:   console.log("[agent] Received message:", data.type, data);
279:   process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
280:   
281:   switch (data.type) {
282:     case "ping":
283:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "pong" }));
284:       break;
285:     case "agent_ready":
286:       console.log("[agent] Agent ready:", data.agentId, "workdir:", data.workdir);
287:       process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
288:       
289:       // Store workdir for relative path computation
290:       const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
291:       if (data.workdir) {
292:         agentService.handleAgentConnect(agentId, ws, data.workdir);
293:       }
294:       
295:       // Start Fossil servers for sessions assigned to this agent
296:       // Note: Sessions are assigned via assignedAgentId, not in agent.sessionIds
297:       const sessions = await sessionRepository.findByAssignedAgentId(agentId);
298:       console.log("[agent] Found", sessions.length, "sessions assigned to agent", agentId);
299:       process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
300:       
301:       if (sessions.length > 0) {
302:         const sessionsReady = [];
303:         const workdir = agentService.getAgentWorkdir(agentId);
304:         console.log("[agent] Workdir:", workdir);
305:         process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
306:         
307:         for (const session of sessions) {
308:           const sessionId = session.id;
309:           console.log("[agent] Session:", sessionId, "status:", session.status);
310:           process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
311:           
312:           if (session.status === "active") {
313:             const fossilPath = `${session.upstreamPath}/../repo.fossil`;
314:             console.log("[agent] Starting fossil server for session:", sessionId, "fossil:", fossilPath);
315:             process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
316:             
317:             const result = await fossilServerManager.startServer(sessionId, fossilPath);
318:             console.log("[agent] Fossil server result:", result);
319:             process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
320:             
321:             if ('port' in result) {
322:               // Update session with port
323:               await sessionRepository.update(sessionId, { port: result.port });
324:               
325:               // Get session with credentials
326:               const sessionWithCreds = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
327:               
328:               sessionsReady.push({
329:                 sessionId,
330:                 port: result.port,
331:                 agentWorkspaceUser: sessionWithCreds?.agentWorkspaceUser,
332:                 agentWorkspacePassword: sessionWithCreds?.agentWorkspacePassword,
333:               });
334:             } else {
335:               console.error("[agent] Failed to start fossil server:", result.error);
336:             }
337:           }
338:         }
339:         
340:         // Send session_ready message to agent
341:         if (sessionsReady.length > 0) {
342:           const message = {
343:             type: 'session_ready',
344:             platformUrl: `http://localhost:${PORT}`,
345:             sessions: sessionsReady,
346:           };
347:           console.log("[agent] Sending session_ready:", JSON.stringify(message));
348:           process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
349:           ws.send(JSON.stringify(message));
350:         } else {
351:           console.log("[agent] No sessions ready to send");
352:         }
353:       } else {
354:         console.log("[agent] No sessions assigned to agent");
355:       }
356:       break;
357:     case "thought_start":
358:       {
359:         const startSessionId = data.sessionId;
360:         if (!startSessionId) {
361:           console.log("No sessionId in thought_start");
362:           return;
363:         }
364:         
365:         // Start new thought buffer
366:         thoughtBuffers.set(startSessionId, "");
367:         
368:         // Forward to clients
369:         const subscribers = chatSessions.get(startSessionId);
370:         if (subscribers) {
371:           subscribers.forEach(client => {
372:             if (client.readyState === 1) {
373:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
374:                 type: data.type,
375:                 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
376:               }));
377:             }
378:           });
379:         }
380:       }
381:       break;
382:       
383:     case "thought_chunk":
384:       {
385:         const chunkSessionId = data.sessionId;
386:         if (!chunkSessionId) {
387:           console.log("No sessionId in thought_chunk");
388:           return;
389:         }
390:         
391:         // Accumulate thought chunks
392:         const currentThoughtBuffer = thoughtBuffers.get(chunkSessionId) || "";
393:         thoughtBuffers.set(chunkSessionId, currentThoughtBuffer + (data.content || ""));
394:         
395:         // Forward to clients
396:         const thoughtChunkSubscribers = chatSessions.get(chunkSessionId);
397:         if (thoughtChunkSubscribers) {
398:           thoughtChunkSubscribers.forEach(client => {
399:             if (client.readyState === 1) {
400:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
401:                 type: data.type,
402:                 content: data.content,
403:                 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
404:               }));
405:             }
406:           });
407:         }
408:       }
409:       break;
410:       
411:     case "thought_end":
412:       {
413:         const endSessionId = data.sessionId;
414:         if (!endSessionId) {
415:           console.log("No sessionId in thought_end");
416:           return;
417:         }
418:         
419:         // Forward to clients
420:         const endSubscribers = chatSessions.get(endSessionId);
421:         if (endSubscribers) {
422:           endSubscribers.forEach(client => {
423:             if (client.readyState === 1) {
424:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
425:                 type: data.type,
426:                 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
427:               }));
428:             }
429:           });
430:         }
431:       }
432:       break;
433:       
434:     case "message_chunk":
435:       {
436:         const msgSessionId = data.sessionId;
437:         if (!msgSessionId) {
438:           console.log("No sessionId in message_chunk");
439:           return;
440:         }
441:         
442:         // Accumulate message chunks
443:         const currentBuffer = streamingBuffers.get(msgSessionId) || "";
444:         streamingBuffers.set(msgSessionId, currentBuffer + (data.content || ""));
445:         
446:         // Forward to clients
447:         const msgSubscribers = chatSessions.get(msgSessionId);
448:         if (msgSubscribers) {
449:           msgSubscribers.forEach(client => {
450:             if (client.readyState === 1) {
451:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
452:                 type: data.type,
453:                 content: data.content,
454:                 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
455:               }));
456:             }
457:           });
458:         }
459:       }
460:       break;
461:       
462:     case "usage_update":
463:       {
464:         const usageSessionId = data.sessionId;
465:         if (!usageSessionId) {
466:           console.log("No sessionId in usage_update");
467:           return;
468:         }
469:         
470:         // Get accumulated message and thoughts
471:         const messageContent = streamingBuffers.get(usageSessionId);
472:         const thoughtContent = thoughtBuffers.get(usageSessionId);
473:         
474:         if (messageContent || thoughtContent) {
475:           // Save assistant response with optional thoughts
476:           let fullContent = messageContent || "";
477:           
478:           // Prepend thoughts if present
479:           if (thoughtContent) {
480:             fullContent = `<details><summary>Thought Process</summary>${thoughtContent}</details>\n\n${fullContent}`;
481:             thoughtBuffers.delete(usageSessionId);
482:           }
483:           
484:           await chatService.saveMessage(usageSessionId, {
485:             role: "assistant",
486:             content: fullContent,
487:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
488:           });
489:           
490:           // Clear buffer
491:           streamingBuffers.delete(usageSessionId);
492:         }
493:         
494:         // Forward usage update to clients
495:         const usageSubscribers = chatSessions.get(usageSessionId);
496:         if (usageSubscribers) {
497:           usageSubscribers.forEach(client => {
498:             if (client.readyState === 1) {
499:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
500:                 type: data.type,
501:                 usage: data.usage,
502:                 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
503:               }));
504:             }
505:           });
506:         }
507:       }
508:       break;
509:     
510:     case "acp_response":
511:       // Legacy: Handle simple ACP response and broadcast to chat
512:       // Agent must specify which session this is for
513:       const sessionId = data.sessionId;
514:       if (!sessionId) {
515:         console.log("No sessionId in acp_response");
516:         return;
517:       }
518:       
519:       // Broadcast to all chat clients in session
520:       const subscribers = chatSessions.get(sessionId);
521:       if (subscribers) {
522:         subscribers.forEach(client => {
523:           if (client.readyState === 1) { // WebSocket.OPEN

527:               content: data.content,
528:               timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
529:             }));

564:       break;

597:         }
598:       }
599:       break;

644: 
645: // Handle chat messages
646: async function handleChatMessage(ws, data) {
647:   const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
648:   
649:   switch (data.type) {
650:     case "send_message":
651:       // Save user message
652:       await chatService.saveMessage(sessionId, {
653:         role: 'user',
654:         content: data.content,
655:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
656:       });
657: 
658:       // Broadcast to all clients in session
659:       const subscribers = chatSessions.get(sessionId);
660:       if (subscribers) {
661:         subscribers.forEach(client => {
662:           if (client.readyState === 1) {
663:             client.send(JSON.stringify({
664:               type: 'message',
665:               role: 'user',
666:               content: data.content,
667:               timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
668:             }));
669:           }
670:         });
671:       }
672: 
673:       // Get session to find assigned agent
674:       const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
675:       if (session?.assignedAgentId) {
676:         // Forward to agent if connected
677:         const ws = agentService.getAgentConnection(session.assignedAgentId);
678:         if (ws && ws.readyState === 1) {
679:           ws.send(JSON.stringify({
680:             type: 'user_message',
681:             sessionId: sessionId,
682:             content: data.content,
683:           }));
684:         }
685:       }
686:       break;
687: 
688:     case "set_model":
689:       // Forward model change to agent
690:       const modelSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
691:       if (modelSession?.assignedAgentId) {
692:         const agentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(modelSession.assignedAgentId);
693:         if (agentWs && agentWs.readyState === 1) {
694:           agentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
695:             type: 'set_model',
696:             sessionId: sessionId,
697:             modelId: data.modelId,
698:           }));
699:         }
700:       }
701:       break;
702: 
703:     case "set_mode":
704:       // Forward mode change to agent
705:       const modeSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
706:       if (modeSession?.assignedAgentId) {
707:         const modeAgentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(modeSession.assignedAgentId);
708:         if (modeAgentWs && modeAgentWs.readyState === 1) {
709:           modeAgentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
710:             type: 'set_mode',
711:             sessionId: sessionId,
712:             modeId: data.modeId,
713:           }));
714:         }
715:       }
716:       break;
717: 
718:     case "request_state":
719:       // Forward state request to agent
720:       const stateSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
721:       if (stateSession?.assignedAgentId) {
722:         const stateAgentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(stateSession.assignedAgentId);
723:         if (stateAgentWs && stateAgentWs.readyState === 1) {
724:           stateAgentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
725:             type: 'request_state',
726:             sessionId: sessionId,
727:           }));
728:         }
729:       }
730:       break;
731:       
732:     case "request_replay":
733:       const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
734:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({
735:         type: 'history',
736:         messages: history,
737:       }));
738:       break;
739:       
740:     default:
741:       console.log("Unknown chat message type:", data.type);
742:   }
743: }
744:
```

```
7: 
8: interface RunningServer {
9:   port: number;
10:   process: ChildProcess;

13: 
14: export class FossilServerManager {
15:   private portsInUse: Set<number> = new Set();
16:   private runningServers: Map<string, RunningServer> = new Map();
17: 
18:   private async isPortAvailable(port: number): Promise<boolean> {
19:     return new Promise((resolve) => {
20:       const socket = createConnection(port, "localhost");
21:       socket.on("connect", () => {
22:         socket.destroy();
23:         resolve(false); // Port is in use
24:       });
25:       socket.on("error", () => {
26:         resolve(true); // Port is available
27:       });
28:     });
29:   }
30: 
31:   async assignPort(): Promise<number | null> {
32:     for (let port = PORT_RANGE_START; port <= PORT_RANGE_END; port++) {
33:       if (!this.portsInUse.has(port)) {
34:         // Check if port is actually available on the system
35:         const available = await this.isPortAvailable(port);
36:         if (available) {
37:           this.portsInUse.add(port);
38:           return port;
39:         }
40:       }
41:     }
42:     return null;
43:   }
44: 
45:   releasePort(port: number): void {
46:     this.portsInUse.delete(port);
47:   }
48: 
49:   async startServer(sessionId: string, repoPath: string): Promise<{ port: number } | { error: string }> {
50:     // Check if server already running for this session
51:     if (this.runningServers.has(sessionId)) {
52:       const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId)!;
53:       return { port: server.port };
54:     }
55: 
56:     // Check if repo exists
57:     if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {
58:       return { error: `Repository not found: ${repoPath}` };
59:     }
60: 
61:     // Assign port
62:     const port = await this.assignPort();
63:     if (port === null) {
64:       return { error: "PORTS_EXHAUSTED" };
65:     }
66: 
67:     // Start fossil server
68:     const proc = spawn("fossil", ["server", "--port", port.toString(), repoPath], {
69:       stdio: "pipe",
70:       detached: false,
71:     });
72: 
73:     // Wait for server to start (simple check: wait a bit)
74:     await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
75: 
76:     // Check if process started successfully
77:     if (proc.pid === undefined) {
78:       this.releasePort(port);
79:       return { error: "Failed to start Fossil server" };
80:     }
81: 
82:     // Track running server
83:     this.runningServers.set(sessionId, {
84:       port,
85:       process: proc,
86:       repoPath,
87:     });
88: 
89:     // Handle process exit
90:     proc.on("exit", () => {
91:       this.runningServers.delete(sessionId);
92:       this.releasePort(port);
93:     });
94: 
95:     return { port };
96:   }
97: 
98:   async stopServer(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
99:     const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId);
100:     if (!server) {
101:       return;
102:     }
103: 
104:     // Kill the process
105:     server.process.kill("SIGTERM");
106: 
107:     // Wait for process to exit
108:     await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
109:       server.process.on("exit", () => {
110:         resolve();
111:       });
112:       // Force kill after 5 seconds
113:       setTimeout(() => {
114:         server.process.kill("SIGKILL");
115:         resolve();
116:       }, 5000);
117:     });
118: 
119:     // Cleanup
120:     this.runningServers.delete(sessionId);
121:     this.releasePort(server.port);
122:   }
123: 
124:   getRunningServer(sessionId: string): { port: number; repoPath: string } | null {
125:     const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId);
126:     if (!server) {
127:       return null;
128:     }
129:     return { port: server.port, repoPath: server.repoPath };
130:   }
131: 
132:   isServerRunning(sessionId: string): boolean {
133:     return this.runningServers.has(sessionId);
134:   }
135: 
136:   getActiveServerCount(): number {

139: 
140:   async stopAllServers(): Promise<void> {

142:       this.stopServer(sessionId)
143:     );
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:29:51)

Done! Committed and pushed successfully.

The fix ensures that when the agent receives a `session_ready` message for an existing checkout, it:
1. Updates the remote URL with new port/credentials
2. Sets the local password
3. Removes the old "server" remote
4. Adds the "server" remote with embedded credentials
5. Performs a sync to verify everything works

This matches exactly what happens during initial clone and resolves the password prompt issue.
## Agent (2026-04-06 23:29:53)

Done! Committed and pushed successfully.

**Summary of the fix:**

When `session_ready` is received for existing checkouts, the agent now applies the same fossil configuration as initial clone:

1. Update remote URL with new port/credentials
2. Set local password to match server
3. Remove old "server" remote: `fossil remote rm server`
4. Add new "server" remote with credentials: `fossil remote add server http://user:pass@host:port/`
5. Verify with sync: `fossil sync server`

This ensures fossil authentication works correctly after server restart.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:31:36)

I see! The JavaScript in `SessionDetailPage.tsx` is polling the `/sessions/{id}/impact` endpoint and storing the data in `window.__impactData`, but the `ImpactBuffer` component is not actually using this data - it's just showing the loading state because `metrics` is undefined.

The issue is that the `ImpactBuffer` component is rendered server-side without metrics, and the client-side JavaScript updates `window.__impactData` but doesn't re-render the component with the new data.

Looking at the code:
1. The `ImpactBuffer` component receives `metrics` as a prop (line 37)
2. If `metrics` is undefined, it shows the loading state (line 211-214)
3. The client-side JavaScript fetches data and stores it in `window.__impactData` but never updates the actual component

There are a few ways to fix this:
1. Make `ImpactBuffer` a client-side component that fetches its own data
2. Have the client-side JavaScript update the DOM directly
3. Use the `window.__impactData` in the component

The simplest fix would be to modify the client-side JavaScript to actually update the DOM when it gets the data. But since the component is already rendered server-side, we need to either:
- Re-render the component with the new data, or
- Update the DOM elements directly

Looking at line 293-296, the JavaScript is storing data in `window.__impactData` but the `updateImpactUI` function only returns early if there's an error or no content - it doesn't actually update the UI!

The issue is that `updateImpactUI` doesn't actually update the DOM - it just stores the data in window variables. 

The ImpactBuffer component remains stuck in its loading state because the metrics prop is never updated. The JavaScript polling mechanism fetches data from the endpoint but only stores it globally without triggering a re-render or DOM update, leaving the component permanently displaying "Calculating impact metrics..."

      <script dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: `
        // Impact metrics polling
        (function() {
          const sessionId = '${session.id}';
          let lastMetrics = null;
          
          async function fetchImpact() {
            try {
              const response = await fetch(\`/sessions/\${sessionId}/impact\`);
              if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch impact');
              
              const data = await response.json();
              updateImpactUI(data);
            } catch (error) {
              console.error('[impact] Polling error:', error);
            }
          }
          
          function updateImpactUI(data) {
            const content = document.getElementById('impact-content');
            if (!content || data.error) return;
            
            // Calculate trends if we have previous data
            let trends = null;
            if (lastMetrics) {
              trends = {
                files: {
                  new: data.files.new > lastMetrics.files.new ? '↑' : data.files.new < lastMetrics.files.new ? '↓' : '→',
                  changed: data.files.changed > lastMetrics.files.changed ? '↑' : data.files.changed < lastMetrics.files.changed ? '↓' : '→',
                  deleted: data.files.deleted > lastMetrics.files.deleted ? '↑' : data.files.deleted < lastMetrics.files.deleted ? '↓' : '→',
                },
                linesOfCode: {
                  added: data.linesOfCode.added > lastMetrics.linesOfCode.added ? '↑' : data.linesOfCode.added < lastMetrics.linesOfCode.added ? '↓' : '→',
                  removed: data.linesOfCode.removed > lastMetrics.linesOfCode.removed ? '↑' : data.linesOfCode.removed < lastMetrics.linesOfCode.removed ? '↓' : '→',
                  net: data.linesOfCode.net > lastMetrics.linesOfCode.net ? '↑' : data.linesOfCode.net < lastMetrics.linesOfCode.net ? '↓' : '→',
                },
                complexity: {
                  cyclomatic: data.complexity.cyclomatic > lastMetrics.complexity.cyclomatic ? '↑' : data.complexity.cyclomatic < lastMetrics.complexity.cyclomatic ? '↓' : '→',
                  cognitive: data.complexity.cognitive > lastMetrics.complexity.cognitive ? '↑' : data.complexity.cognitive < lastMetrics.complexity.cognitive ? '↓' : '→',
                }
              };
            }
            
            lastMetrics = data;

          fetchImpact();
newString:       {/* Impact Polling Script */}
      <script dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: `
        // Impact metrics polling
        (function() {
          const sessionId = '${session.id}';
          let lastMetrics = null;
          
          function formatTrend(current, previous) {
            if (current > previous) return '↑';
            if (current < previous) return '↓';
            return '→';
          }
          
          function formatComplexity(value) {
            if (value > 0) return '+' + value;
            return value.toString();
          }
          
          function formatTime(minutes) {
            if (minutes < 60) return minutes + 'm';
            const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);
            const mins = minutes % 60;
            return hours + 'h ' + mins + 'm';
          }
          
          async function fetchImpact() {
            try {
              const response = await fetch(\`/sessions/\${sessionId}/impact\`);
              if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch impact');
              
              const data = await response.json();
              updateImpactUI(data);
            } catch (error) {
              console.error('[impact] Polling error:', error);
            }
          }
          
          function updateImpactUI(data) {
            const content = document.getElementById('impact-content');
            if (!content || data.error) return;
            
            // Store for trend comparison
            const prevMetrics = lastMetrics;
            lastMetrics = data;
            
            // Build HTML for metrics
            let html = '';
            
            // Files Section
            const filesTrend = prevMetrics ? {
              new: formatTrend(data.files.new, prevMetrics.files.new),
              changed: formatTrend(data.files.changed, prevMetrics.files.changed),
              deleted: formatTrend(data.files.deleted, prevMetrics.files.deleted)
            } : { new: '→', changed: '→', deleted: '→' };
            
            html += \`<div class="impact-section">
              <div class="impact-section-title">Files</div>
              <div class="impact-metric">
                <span class="impact-metric-label">New:</span>
                <span class="impact-metric-value">\${data.files.new}</span>
                <span class="impact-trend">\${filesTrend.new}</span>
              </div>
              <div class="impact-metric">
                <span class="impact-metric-label">Changed:</span>
                <span class="impact-metric-value">\${data.files.changed}</span>
                <span class="impact-trend">\${filesTrend.changed}</span>
              </div>
              <div class="impact-metric">
                <span class="impact-metric-label">Deleted:</span>
                <span class="impact-metric-value">\${data.files.deleted}</span>
                <span class="impact-trend">\${filesTrend.deleted}</span>
              </div>
            </div>\`;
            
            // Lines of Code Section
            const locTrend = prevMetrics ? {
              added: formatTrend(data.linesOfCode.added, prevMetrics.linesOfCode.added),
              removed: formatTrend(data.linesOfCode.removed, prevMetrics.linesOfCode.removed),
              net: formatTrend(data.linesOfCode.net, prevMetrics.linesOfCode.net)
            } : { added: '→', removed: '→', net: '→' };
            
            const netClass = data.linesOfCode.net >= 0 ? 'positive' : 'negative';
            const netPrefix = data.linesOfCode.net >= 0 ? '+' : '';
            
            html += \`<div class="impact-section">
              <div class="impact-section-title">Lines of Code</div>
              <div class="impact-metric">
                <span class="impact-metric-label">Added:</span>
                <span class="impact-metric-value">+\${data.linesOfCode.added}</span>
                <span class="impact-trend">\${locTrend.added}</span>
              </div>
              <div class="impact-metric">
                <span class="impact-metric-label">Removed:</span>
                <span class="impact-metric-value">-\${data.linesOfCode.removed}</span>
                <span class="impact-trend">\${locTrend.removed}</span>
              </div>
              <div class="impact-metric">
                <span class="impact-metric-label">Net:</span>
                <span class="impact-metric-value \${netClass}">\${netPrefix}\${data.linesOfCode.net}</span>
                <span class="impact-trend">\${locTrend.net}</span>
              </div>
            </div>\`;
            
            // Complexity Section (if scc is installed)
            if (data.sccInstalled !== false && data.complexity) {
              const compTrend = prevMetrics ? {
                cyclomatic: formatTrend(data.complexity.cyclomatic, prevMetrics.complexity.cyclomatic),
                cognitive: formatTrend(data.complexity.cognitive, prevMetrics.complexity.cognitive)
              } : { cyclomatic: '→', cognitive: '→' };
              
              html += \`<div class="impact-section">
                <div class="impact-section-title">Complexity</div>
                <div class="impact-metric">
                  <span class="impact-metric-label">Cyclomatic:</span>
                  <span class="impact-metric-value">\${formatComplexity(data.complexity.cyclomatic)}</span>
                  <span class="impact-trend">\${compTrend.cyclomatic}</span>
                </div>
                <div class="impact-metric">
                  <span class="impact-metric-label">Cognitive:</span>
                  <span class="impact-metric-value">\${formatComplexity(data.complexity.cognitive)}</span>
                  <span class="impact-trend">\${compTrend.cognitive}</span>
                </div>
                <div class="impact-metric">
                  <span class="impact-metric-label">Est. Time:</span>
                  <span class="impact-metric-value">\${formatTime(data.complexity.estimatedMinutes)}</span>
                </div>
              </div>\`;
              
              // Language Breakdown
              if (data.byLanguage && data.byLanguage.length > 0) {
                html += \`<div class="impact-section">
                  <div class="impact-section-title">By Language</div>\`;
                data.byLanguage.forEach(lang => {
                  const added = lang.linesAdded > 0 ? '+' + lang.linesAdded : '';
                  const removed = lang.linesRemoved > 0 ? ' -' + lang.linesRemoved : '';
                  const complexity = lang.complexityDelta !== 0 ? 
                    ' (' + (lang.complexityDelta > 0 ? '+' : '') + lang.complexityDelta + ' cyc)' : '';
                  html += \`<div class="impact-language">
                    <div class="impact-language-header">
                      <span class="impact-language-name">\${lang.language}</span>
                      <span class="impact-language-files">\${lang.files} files</span>
                    </div>
                    <div class="impact-language-metrics">
                      <span class="impact-language-loc">\${added}\${removed}</span>
                      <span class="impact-language-complexity">\${complexity}</span>
                    </div>
                  </div>\`;
                });
                html += '</div>';
              }
            }
            
            // Update the content
            content.innerHTML = html;
          }
          
          // Poll every 5 seconds
          setInterval(fetchImpact, 5000);
          
          // Initial fetch
          fetchImpact();

```
+                <span class="impact-trend">\${filesTrend.new}</span>
+              </div>
+              <div class="impact-metric">
+                <span class="impact-metric-label">Changed:</span>
+                <span class="impact-metric-value">\${data.files.changed}</span>
+                <span class="impact-trend">\${filesTrend.changed}</span>
+              </div>
+              <div class="impact-metric">
+                <span class="impact-metric-label">Deleted:</span>
+                <span class="impact-metric-value">\${data.files.deleted}</span>
+                <span class="impact-trend">\${filesTrend.deleted}</span>
+              </div>
+            </div>\`;
+            
+            // Lines of Code Section
+            const locTrend = prevMetrics ? {
+              added: formatTrend(data.linesOfCode.added, prevMetrics.linesOfCode.added),
+              removed: formatTrend(data.linesOfCode.removed, prevMetrics.linesOfCode.removed),
+              net: formatTrend(data.linesOfCode.net, prevMetrics.linesOfCode.net)
+            } : { added: '→', removed: '→', net: '→' };
+            
+            const netClass = data.linesOfCode.net >= 0 ? 'positive' : 'negative';
+            const netPrefix = data.linesOfCode.net >= 0 ? '+' : '';
+            
+            html += \`<div class="impact-section">
+              <div class="impact-section-title">Lines of Code</div>
+              <div class="impact-metric">
+                <span class="impact-metric-label">Added:</span>
+                <span class="impact-metric-value">+\${data.linesOfCode.added}</span>
+                <span class="impact-trend">\${locTrend.added}</span>
+              </div>
+              <div class="impact-metric">
+                <span class="impact-metric-label">Removed:</span>
+                <span class="impact-metric-value">-\${data.linesOfCode.removed}</span>
+                <span class="impact-trend">\${locTrend.removed}</span>
+              </div>
+              <div class="impact-metric">
+                <span class="impact-metric-label">Net:</span>
+                <span class="impact-metric-value \${netClass}">\${netPrefix}\${data.linesOfCode.net}</span>
+                <span class="impact-trend">\${locTrend.net}</span>
+              </div>
+            </div>\`;
+            
+            // Complexity Section (if scc is installed)
+            if (data.sccInstalled !== false && data.complexity) {
+              const compTrend = prevMetrics ? {
+                cyclomatic: formatTrend(data.complexity.cyclomatic, prevMetrics.complexity.cyclomatic),
+                cognitive: formatTrend(data.complexity.cognitive, prevMetrics.complexity.cognitive)
+              } : { cyclomatic: '→', cognitive: '→' };
+              
+              html += \`<div class="impact-section">
+                <div class="impact-section-title">Complexity</div>
+                <div class="impact-metric">
+                  <span class="impact-metric-label">Cyclomatic:</span>
+                  <span class="impact-metric-value">\${formatComplexity(data.complexity.cyclomatic)}</span>
+                  <span class="impact-trend">\${compTrend.cyclomatic}</span>
+                </div>
+                <div class="impact-metric">
+                  <span class="impact-metric-label">Cognitive:</span>
+                  <span class="impact-metric-value">\${formatComplexity(data.complexity.cognitive)}</span>
+                  <span class="impact-trend">\${compTrend.cognitive}</span>
+                </div>
+                <div class="impact-metric">
+                  <span class="impact-metric-label">Est. Time:</span>
+                  <span class="impact-metric-value">\${formatTime(data.complexity.estimatedMinutes)}</span>
+                </div>
+              </div>\`;
+              
+              // Language Breakdown
+              if (data.byLanguage && data.byLanguage.length > 0) {
+                html += \`<div class="impact-section">
+                  <div class="impact-section-title">By Language</div>\`;
+                data.byLanguage.forEach(lang => {
+                  const added = lang.linesAdded > 0 ? '+' + lang.linesAdded : '';
+                  const removed = lang.linesRemoved > 0 ? ' -' + lang.linesRemoved : '';
+                  const complexity = lang.complexityDelta !== 0 ? 
+                    ' (' + (lang.complexityDelta > 0 ? '+' : '') + lang.complexityDelta + ' cyc)' : '';
+                  html += \`<div class="impact-language">
+                    <div class="impact-language-header">
+                      <span class="impact-language-name">\${lang.language}</span>
+                      <span class="impact-language-files">\${lang.files} files</span>
+                    </div>
+                    <div class="impact-language-metrics">
+                      <span class="impact-language-loc">\${added}\${removed}</span>
+                      <span class="impact-language-complexity">\${complexity}</span>
+                    </div>
+                  </div>\`;
+                });
+                html += '</div>';
+              }
+            }
+            
+            // Update the content
+            content.innerHTML = html;
           }
           
           // Poll every 5 seconds
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:32:48)

Done! The fix has been committed and pushed.

**Summary of the fix:**

The issue was that the JavaScript in `SessionDetailPage.tsx` was fetching impact metrics from the API but never updating the DOM. The `updateImpactUI` function only stored data in `window.__impactData` and `window.__impactTrends` variables but didn't actually render anything.

**Fixed by:**
- Building HTML strings for each section (Files, Lines of Code, Complexity, By Language)
- Calculating trend indicators (up/down arrows)
- Formatting values (complexity deltas, estimated time)
- Setting `content.innerHTML = html` to actually update the DOM

Now when you load a session page:
1. Initial fetch happens immediately
2. The impact buffer shows the actual metrics instead of "Calculating..."
3. Updates every 5 seconds with trend indicators showing changes
4. Properly handles the case when scc is not installed## User (2026-04-06 23:32:53)

## Agent (2026-04-06 23:35:06)

The fix has been committed and pushed. The platform is running with `--watch` mode which should auto-reload the changes automatically.

**Summary of the fix:**

The JavaScript in `SessionDetailPage.tsx` now properly updates the DOM after fetching impact metrics. Previously it only stored data in `window.__impactData` but never rendered it.

**What changed:**
- Added `updateImpactUI()` function that builds HTML for:
  - Files section (new, changed, deleted with trends)
  - Lines of Code section (added, removed, net with trends)
  - Complexity section (cyclomatic, cognitive, estimated time)
  - Language breakdown by file type
- Updates `content.innerHTML` with the rendered metrics

**To verify it's working:**
1. Navigate to a session page
2. The impact buffer should show actual metrics instead of "Calculating..."
3. It auto-refreshes every 5 seconds

If you're still seeing "Calculating impact metrics...", try refreshing the browser page or check the browser console (F12 → Console) for any JavaScript errors.

````
<skill_content name="llm-vcs-session-saver">
# Skill: llm-vcs-session-saver

# LLM VCS Session Saver

Saves an LLM conversation session as a flat text file optimized for version control: one line per message, unique timestamped filename, no identifying information. Works with any VCS — Git, SVN, Mercurial, Perforce, and others.

---

## Output File Path

Save the session file to:

```
llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
```

- `YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS` — timestamp of when the session was saved (UTC)
- `XXXX` — 4-character random alphanumeric string to prevent collisions
- Do **not** include usernames, email addresses, or any identifying info in the filename or content

Example: `llm-sessions/2025-04-01-143022-k7qr-session.txt`

---

## Content Format

Each message must be written as a **single line** in this exact format:

```
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] User: <message text>
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] LLM: <message text>
```

### Rules

- **Chronological order** — messages appear in the order they occurred
- **Timestamps** — use `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` format (UTC preferred)
- **Speaker labels** — only `User:` and `LLM:` (no other labels or roles)
- **One line per message** — newlines within a message must be replaced with a space or `\n` literal so each message stays on one line
- **No summarizing** — reproduce messages fully and verbatim
- **No extra formatting** — no markdown, no section headers, no blank lines between messages
- **No metadata block** — do not prepend titles, model names, or session IDs

### Example Output

```
[2025-04-01 14:30:01] User: What is the capital of France?
[2025-04-01 14:30:03] LLM: The capital of France is Paris.
[2025-04-01 14:30:10] User: And what's the population?
[2025-04-01 14:30:12] LLM: Paris has a population of approximately 2.1 million in the city proper.
```

---

## Git Readiness

- The file can be written directly and committed with no post-processing
- One-line-per-message ensures Git diffs are clean and human-readable — each changed message shows as exactly one modified line
- The `llm-sessions/` directory should be committed as part of the repo (not gitignored)

---

## Commit Workflow

Every time the user asks to make a Git commit — for any reason, even if unrelated to sessions — run this skill first, then include the session file in the commit.

Steps to follow on every commit:

1. **Generate the session file** using the path format and content rules above
2. **Stage the session file** along with the user's other changes:
   ```bash
   git add llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
   git add <any other files the user wants to commit>
   ```
3. **Commit everything together**:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "<user's commit message>"
   ```

The session file should reflect the full conversation up to and including the commit request itself.

---

## Multi-Session Safety

- Filenames are unique by design (timestamp + random ID), so concurrent sessions will never overwrite each other
- No locking or coordination needed between sessions
- If two sessions start in the same second, the random suffix (`XXXX`) prevents collision

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/.config/opencode/skills/session-history
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:41:30)

The issue is that inside the template literal (using backticks), the `${}` syntax is trying to interpolate the variable `data` at the JSX level (server-side rendering time), but `data` is only available at runtime in the browser JavaScript. The template literal is inside a `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` which is a string that will be executed in the browser.

The fix is to escape the template literal interpolation in the JSX string, or use a different approach. Let me fix this properly.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:42:00)

Done! Fixed the template literal issue. The problem was that `${data.warning}` inside the `\`...\`` template literal was being evaluated by JSX during server-side rendering, not by JavaScript in the browser. I changed it to string concatenation to avoid the conflict.
## Agent (2026-04-06 23:42:00)

Done! Fixed the template literal issue.

**The problem:** Inside the `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` block, using `\`${data.warning}\`` caused JSX to try to evaluate `data` as a prop during server-side rendering, which doesn't exist at that scope.

**The fix:** Changed to string concatenation using `+` operators instead of nested template literals. This way the JavaScript code is passed verbatim to the browser and executes there where `data` is available.

              const result = await response.json();
              
              if (result.success) {
                window.location.reload();
              } else {
                alert('Failed to install scc: ' + (result.error || 'Unknown error'));
                if (btn) {
                  btn.textContent = 'Install scc';
                  btn.disabled = false;
                }
              }
            } catch (error) {
              alert('Failed to install scc: ' + error.message);
              if (btn) {
                btn.textContent = 'Install scc';
                btn.disabled = false;
              }
            }
          };
        })();
newString:           // Poll every 5 seconds
          setInterval(fetchImpact, 5000);
          
          // Initial fetch
          fetchImpact();
        })();

filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-platform/src/components/SessionDetailPage.tsx
newString:           function updateImpactUI(data) {
            const content = document.getElementById('impact-content');
            if (!content) return;
            
            // Check for API errors
            if (data.error) {
              console.error('[impact] API error:', data.error);
              content.innerHTML = '<div class="impact-loading"><p>Error loading impact metrics: ' + data.error + '</p></div>';
              return;
            }
            
            // Check if we have valid data structure
            if (!data.files || typeof data.files !== 'object') {
              console.error('[impact] Invalid data structure:', data);
              content.innerHTML = '<div class="impact-loading"><p>Error: Invalid impact data received</p></div>';
              return;
            }
            
            // Store for trend comparison
            const prevMetrics = lastMetrics;
            lastMetrics = data;
            
            // Build HTML for metrics
            let html = '';
            
            // Warning if scc not installed
            if (data.sccInstalled === false) {
              const warningMsg = data.warning || 'scc not installed - complexity metrics unavailable';
              html += '<div class="impact-warning" style="margin-bottom: 12px; padding: 10px; background: #332b1a; border: 1px solid #665a33; border-radius: 4px;">' +
                '<div style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 8px;">' +
                '<span style="font-size: 16px;">⚠️</span>' +
                '<span style="flex: 1; color: #d4a040;">' + warningMsg + '</span>' +
                '</div>' +
                '<div style="font-size: 11px; color: #888; padding-left: 24px;">' +
                'Install scc from <a href="https://github.com/boyter/scc" target="_blank" style="color: #6ea8fe;">github.com/boyter/scc</a> and place the binary at: ~/.mimo/bin/scc' +
                '</div>' +
                '</div>';
              
              // Log to console where user should put scc
              console.log('[mimo] SCC feature disabled. To enable impact complexity metrics, install scc from https://github.com/boyter/scc and place the binary at: ~/.mimo/bin/scc');
            }
            
            // Files Section
            const filesTrend = prevMetrics ? {
              new: formatTrend(data.files.new, prevMetrics.files.new),
              changed: formatTrend(data.files.changed, prevMetrics.files.changed),
              deleted: formatTrend(data.files.deleted, prevMetrics.files.deleted)
            } : { new: '→', changed: '→', deleted: '→' };

oldString:           function updateImpactUI(data) {
            const content = document.getElementById('impact-content');
            if (!content) return;
            
            // Check for API errors
            if (data.error) {
              console.error('[impact] API error:', data.error);
              content.innerHTML = '<div class="impact-loading"><p>Error loading impact metrics: ' + data.error + '</p></div>';
              return;
            }
            
            // Check if we have valid data structure
            if (!data.files || typeof data.files !== 'object') {
              console.error('[impact] Invalid data structure:', data);
              content.innerHTML = '<div class="impact-loading"><p>Error: Invalid impact data received</p></div>';
              return;
            }
newString:           function updateImpactUI(data) {
            const content = document.getElementById('impact-content');
            if (!content) return;
            
            // Check for API errors
            if (data.error) {
              console.error('[impact] API error:', data.error);
              content.innerHTML = '<div class="impact-loading"><p>Error loading impact metrics: ' + data.error + '</p></div>';
              return;
            }
            
            // Normalize data structure - handle both {files, linesOfCode...} and {metrics: {...}} formats
            const metrics = data.metrics || data;
            
            // Check if we have valid data structure
            if (!metrics.files || typeof metrics.files !== 'object') {
              console.error('[impact] Invalid data structure:', data);
              content.innerHTML = '<div class="impact-loading"><p>Error: Invalid impact data received</p></div>';
              return;
            }
            
            // Use normalized metrics for all access
            data = metrics;

```
56:     return join(this.getSessionPath(projectId, sessionId), "upstream");
57:   }

60:     return join(this.getSessionPath(projectId, sessionId), "agent-workspace");

63:   private generateId(): string {
64:     return crypto.randomUUID();
65:   }
66: 
67:   async create(input: CreateSessionInput): Promise<Session> {
68:     const id = this.generateId();
69:     const sessionPath = this.getSessionPath(input.projectId, id);
70:     const upstreamPath = this.getUpstreamPath(input.projectId, id);
71:     const agentWorkspacePath = this.getAgentWorkspacePath(input.projectId, id);
72: 
73:     // Create session directory
74:     if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
75:       mkdirSync(sessionPath, { recursive: true });
76:     }
77: 
78:     // Create upstream directory
79:     if (!existsSync(upstreamPath)) {
80:       mkdirSync(upstreamPath, { recursive: true });
81:     }
82: 
83:     // Create agent-workspace directory
84:     if (!existsSync(agentWorkspacePath)) {
85:       mkdirSync(agentWorkspacePath, { recursive: true });
86:     }
87: 
88:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
89:     const sessionData: SessionData = {
90:       id,
91:       name: input.name,
92:       projectId: input.projectId,
93:       owner: input.owner,
94:       upstreamPath,
95:       agentWorkspacePath,
96:       assignedAgentId: input.assignedAgentId,
97:       status: "active",
98:       port: null,
99:       createdAt: now,
100:       updatedAt: now,
101:     };
102: 
103:     writeFileSync(
104:       this.getSessionFilePath(input.projectId, id),
105:       dump(sessionData),
106:       "utf-8"
107:     );
108: 
109:     return {
110:       ...sessionData,
111:       createdAt: new Date(sessionData.createdAt),
112:       updatedAt: new Date(sessionData.updatedAt),
113:     };
114:   }
115: 
116:   async findById(sessionId: string): Promise<Session | null> {
117:     // Search across all projects for the session
118:     const Paths = getPaths();
119:     if (!existsSync(Paths.projects)) {
120:       return null;
121:     }
122: 
123:     const projectEntries = readdirSync(Paths.projects, { withFileTypes: true });
124:     
125:     for (const projectEntry of projectEntries) {
126:       if (projectEntry.isDirectory()) {
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:58:09)

I see now! The agent:
1. Receives the `session_ready` message with the port
2. The `fossilUrl` is built from `platformHost:${port}`
3. The agent clones/opens the repository from that URL

But the issue is that the **platform starts the fossil server for `upstream/../repo.fossil`**, not for `agent-workspace/`. The agent then clones from that server.

Looking at the session info you showed:
```
fossil server --port 8020 /home/bit4bit/.mimo/.../sessions/c28a0c8f.../upstream/../repo.fossil
```

The server is serving `repo.fossil` (which is at the session level, not upstream level). The `../` goes from `upstream/` to the session directory, then to `repo.fossil`.

So the server is serving the fossil repository that was created from the upstream import.

The agent then clones from this server into its workDir (`/tmp/demo/...`).

The impact calculator then compares:
- `upstream/` - the original git repo files
- `agentWorkspacePath/` - which is `.mimo/.../agent-workspace/`

But the agent is making changes in `/tmp/demo/.../`, NOT in `.mimo/.../agent-workspace/`.

This is the bug! The impact calculator is looking at the wrong directory. It should be looking at where the agent actually made changes.

But wait, the fossil server is serving `repo.fossil`, and when the agent pushes, it pushes to that repo. So the changes should end up in `repo.fossil`.

But the impact calculator compares `upstream/` (original files) with `agentWorkspacePath/` (`.mimo/.../agent-workspace/`).

The architecture should be:
1. User creates session → platform creates `upstream/` (git clone), `agent-workspace/` (empty), and `repo.fossil` (fossil repo from upstream)


2. Platform starts fossil server for `repo.fossil`
3. Agent clones from fossil server into its workdir (`/tmp/demo/.../`)
4. Agent makes changes and pushes back to `repo.fossil`
5. Platform syncs `repo.fossil` to `agent-workspace/` periodically or on commit
6. Impact calculator compares `upstream/` with `agentWorkspacePath/`

The problem is step 5 - the `agentWorkspacePath` (`.mimo/.../agent-workspace/`) is not being synced with the changes. The changes are only in `repo.fossil` and in the agent's workdir (`/tmp/demo/.../`).

So the impact calculator shows no changes because `agentWorkspacePath/` is empty or has the original files, not the modified ones.

The platform needs to either:
1. Sync `repo.fossil` to `agent-workspace/` before calculating impact
2. Or compare `upstream/` with `repo.fossil` directly instead of `agentWorkspacePath/`

Looking at the commit service, it seems to sync `agent-workspace/` with `repo.fossil` during the commit process. But for real-time impact, we need to ensure `agentWorkspacePath/` reflects the current state. The commit service already handles syncing `agent-workspace/` with `repo.fossil`, but real-time impact calculation needs that sync to happen before metrics are calculated.

The impact calculator is comparing `upstream/` with `agentWorkspacePath/`, which never gets updated with the agent's changes since those live in `/tmp/demo/.../`. When the agent pushes to `repo.fossil`, those changes don't automatically propagate to `agentWorkspacePath/`. To fix this, we'd need to either update `agentWorkspacePath/` before calculating impact, or change the calculator to compare `upstream/` with `repo.fossil` directly instead.

```
404:       // Return basic file counts without complexity
405:       const { readdirSync, statSync, readFileSync } = await import("fs");
406:       const { join, relative } = await import("path");
407:       const crypto = await import("crypto");
408: 
409:       const scanDir = (dir: string, baseDir: string, files: Map<string, { checksum: string; size: number }>) => {
410:         if (!statSync(dir, { throwIfNoEntry: false })) return;
411:         const entries = readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
412:         for (const entry of entries) {
413:           const fullPath = join(dir, entry.name);
414:           const relPath = relative(baseDir, fullPath);
415:           if (entry.name.startsWith(".")) continue;
416:           if (entry.isDirectory()) {
417:             scanDir(fullPath, baseDir, files);
418:           } else {
419:             const stats = statSync(fullPath);
420:             const content = readFileSync(fullPath);
421:             const checksum = crypto.createHash("md5").update(content).digest("hex");
422:             files.set(relPath, { checksum, size: stats.size });
423:           }
424:         }
425:       };
426: 
427:       const upstreamFiles = new Map<string, { checksum: string; size: number }>();
428:       const workspaceFiles = new Map<string, { checksum: string; size: number }>();

430:       scanDir(session.upstreamPath, session.upstreamPath, upstreamFiles);
431:       scanDir(session.agentWorkspacePath, session.agentWorkspacePath, workspaceFiles);
432: 
433:       let newCount = 0, changedCount = 0, deletedCount = 0;
434: 
435:       for (const [path, info] of workspaceFiles) {
436:         if (!upstreamFiles.has(path)) newCount++;
437:         else if (upstreamFiles.get(path)?.checksum !== info.checksum) changedCount++;
438:       }
439: 
440:       for (const [path] of upstreamFiles) {
441:         if (!workspaceFiles.has(path)) deletedCount++;

443:
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-06 23:59:14)

Done! I've added a sync step before calculating impact. Now:

1. Check if `repo.fossil` exists
2. If yes, check if `agentWorkspacePath` has a fossil checkout (`.fslckout` file)
3. If not, initialize the checkout using `vcs.openFossil()`
4. Run `fossil up` to sync the latest changes from `repo.fossil`

This ensures that `agentWorkspacePath` always has the latest changes that the agent pushed, so the impact calculation compares:
- `upstream/` (original git repo)  
- `agentWorkspacePath/` (synced with repo.fossil which has agent's changes)
## Agent (2026-04-06 23:59:16)

Done! I've added a sync step before calculating impact. Now:

1. Check if `repo.fossil` exists
2. If yes, check if `agentWorkspacePath` has a fossil checkout (`.fslckout` file)
3. If not, initialize the checkout using `vcs.openFossil()`
4. Run `fossil up` to sync the latest changes from `repo.fossil`

This ensures that `agentWorkspacePath` always has the latest changes that the agent pushed, so the impact calculation compares:
- `upstream/` (original git repo)  
- `agentWorkspacePath/` (synced with repo.fossil which has agent's changes)

Now refresh the page and the impact metrics should show the changes you made in the agent's checkout.

```
183:   async runScc(directory: string): Promise<SccMetrics> {
184:     // Check cache first
185:     const cacheKey = directory;
186:     const cached = this.cache.get(cacheKey);
187:     if (cached && Date.now() - cached.timestamp < this.cacheTTL) {
188:       return cached.data;
189:     }
190: 
191:     if (!this.isInstalled()) {
192:       throw new Error("scc is not installed. Run install() first.");
193:     }
194: 
195:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
196:       const args = ["--by-file", "-f", "json", directory];
197:       const child = spawn(this.sccPath, args, {
198:         timeout: 30000, // 30 second timeout
199:       });
200: 
201:       let stdout = "";
202:       let stderr = "";
203: 
204:       child.stdout.on("data", (data) => {
205:         stdout += data.toString();
206:       });
207: 
208:       child.stderr.on("data", (data) => {
209:         stderr += data.toString();
210:       });
211: 
212:       child.on("close", (code) => {
213:         if (code !== 0) {
214:           reject(new Error(`scc exited with code ${code}: ${stderr}`));
215:           return;
216:         }
217: 
218:         try {
219:           const output: SccJsonOutput = JSON.parse(stdout);
220:           const metrics = this.parseSccOutput(output);
221:           
222:           // Cache the result
223:           this.cache.set(cacheKey, { data: metrics, timestamp: Date.now() });
224:           
225:           resolve(metrics);
226:         } catch (error) {
227:           reject(new Error(`Failed to parse scc output: ${error}`));
228:         }
229:       });
230: 
231:       child.on("error", (error) => {
232:         reject(new Error(`Failed to run scc: ${error.message}`));
233:       });
234:     });
235:   }
```

I see the issue now. Looking at the code flow:

1. `calculateImpact()` calls `sccService.runScc(upstreamPath)` and `sccService.runScc(agentWorkspacePath)`
2. `runScc()` uses caching based on the directory path
3. The cache has a TTL of 5 minutes (300 seconds)

The problem is likely that:
1. The cache is returning stale data from before the sync
2. Or the scc output for the new file isn't being parsed correctly

```
+    Language: string;
+    PossibleLanguages: string[];
+    Filename: string;
+    Extension: string;
+    Location: string;
+    Symlocation: string;
+    Bytes: number;
+    Lines: number;
+    Code: number;
+    Comment: number;
+    Blank: number;
+    Complexity: number;
+    WeightedComplexity: number;
+    Hash: string | null;
+    Binary: boolean;
+    Minified: boolean;
+    Generated: boolean;
+    EndPoint: number;
+    Uloc: number;
   }>;
-}
\ No newline at end of file
+  LineLength: null;
+  ULOC: number;
+}
+
+type SccJsonOutput = SccLanguageGroup[];
\ No newline at end of file
```

newString:   private parseSccOutput(output: SccJsonOutput): SccMetrics {
    // SCC returns an array of language groups, each containing files
    const languageGroups = Array.isArray(output) ? output : [];
    
    // Calculate totals
    let totalLines = 0;
    let totalCode = 0;
    let totalComplexity = 0;
    
    const languageMap = new Map<string, SccLanguageMetrics>();
    const fileMetrics: SccFileMetrics[] = [];

    // Process each language group
    for (const group of languageGroups) {
      const files = group.Files || [];
      
      for (const file of files) {
        totalLines += file.Lines;
        totalCode += file.Code;
        totalComplexity += file.Complexity || 0;

        // Language aggregation
        const lang = file.Language || "Unknown";
        const existing = languageMap.get(lang);
        if (existing) {
          existing.files++;
          existing.lines += file.Lines;
          existing.code += file.Code;
          existing.comment += file.Comment;
          existing.blank += file.Blank;
          existing.complexity += file.Complexity || 0;
        } else {
          languageMap.set(lang, {
            language: lang,
            files: 1,
            lines: file.Lines,
            code: file.Code,
            comment: file.Comment,
            blank: file.Blank,
            complexity: file.Complexity || 0,
          });
        }

        fileMetrics.push({
          path: file.Filename,
          language: lang,
          lines: file.Lines,
          code: file.Code,
          comment: file.Comment,
          blank: file.Blank,
          complexity: file.Complexity || 0,
        });
      }
    }

    return {
      linesOfCode: {
        added: totalCode, // Will be calculated as delta by ImpactCalculator
        removed: 0,
        net: totalCode,
      },
      complexity: {
        cyclomatic: totalComplexity,
        cognitive: 0, // scc doesn't provide cognitive complexity
        estimatedMinutes: Math.ceil(totalCode / 10), // Rough estimate: 10 lines per minute
      },
      byLanguage: Array.from(languageMap.values()),
      byFile: fileMetrics,
    };
  }
oldString:   private parseSccOutput(output: SccJsonOutput): SccMetrics {
    const files = output.files || [];
    
    // Calculate totals
    let totalLines = 0;
    let totalCode = 0;
    let totalComplexity = 0;
    
    const languageMap = new Map<string, SccLanguageMetrics>();
    const fileMetrics: SccFileMetrics[] = [];

    for (const file of files) {
      totalLines += file.lines;
      totalCode += file.code;
      totalComplexity += file.complexity || 0;

      // Language aggregation
      const lang = file.language || "Unknown";
      const existing = languageMap.get(lang);
      if (existing) {
        existing.files++;
        existing.lines += file.lines;
        existing.code += file.code;
        existing.comment += file.comment;
        existing.blank += file.blank;
        existing.complexity += file.complexity || 0;
      } else {
        languageMap.set(lang, {
          language: lang,
          files: 1,
          lines: file.lines,
          code: file.code,
          comment: file.comment,
          blank: file.blank,
          complexity: file.complexity || 0,
        });
      }

      fileMetrics.push({
        path: file.filename,
        language: lang,
        lines: file.lines,
        code: file.code,
        comment: file.comment,
        blank: file.blank,
        complexity: file.complexity || 0,
      });
    }

    return {
      linesOfCode: {
        added: totalCode, // Will be calculated as delta by ImpactCalculator
        removed: 0,
        net: totalCode,
      },
      complexity: {
        cyclomatic: totalComplexity,
        cognitive: 0, // scc doesn't provide cognitive complexity
        estimatedMinutes: Math.ceil(totalCode / 10), // Rough estimate: 10 lines per minute
      },
      byLanguage: Array.from(languageMap.values()),
      byFile: fileMetrics,
    };
  }

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-07 00:10:02)

Done! I've fixed the scc JSON parser. The issue was:

**Before:** Expected `{"files": [...]}` structure

**Now:** Correctly parses the array format `[{"Name": "...", "Files": [...]}]`

The changes were:
1. Updated the `SccJsonOutput` interface to match scc's actual output format (array of language groups)
2. Updated `parseSccOutput` to iterate through language groups and extract files from the `Files` property (capital F)
3. Fixed property names to use PascalCase (e.g., `file.Filename`, `file.Language`, `file.Lines`)

Now the impact metrics should correctly show lines of code and complexity data.

2. Updated `parseSccOutput` to iterate through language groups and extract files from the `Files` property (capital F)
3. Fixed property names to use PascalCase (e.g., `file.Filename`, `file.Language`, `file.Lines`)

Now the impact metrics should correctly show lines of code and complexity data.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-07 00:12:43)

Done! Committed and pushed.

The fix corrects the scc JSON parser to match the actual output format from scc:
- SCC returns: `[{"Name": "Python", "Files": [...]}, {"Name": "Markdown", "Files": [...]}]`
- Parser was expecting: `{"files": [...]}`

Updated both the interface definition and the parser logic to correctly extract metrics from the nested structure.
## Agent (2026-04-07 00:12:44)

Done! Committed and pushed.

**Summary of the fix:**

The scc JSON parser was expecting the wrong format:
- **Expected:** `{"files": [{filename, language, lines, code, ...}]}`
- **Actual scc output:** `[{"Name": "Python", "Files": [{Filename, Language, Lines, Code, ...}]}, ...]`

Fixed by:
1. Updating `SccJsonOutput` interface to match the array-of-language-groups format
2. Iterating through language groups and extracting files from nested `Files` arrays
3. Using PascalCase property names (`Filename`, `Language`, `Lines`, `Code`, etc.)

newString:   describe("SccService", () => {
    it("should detect platform correctly", async () => {
      const { SccService } = await import("../src/impact/scc-service.ts");
      const service = new SccService(join(testHome, "bin", "scc"));
      const platform = service.detectPlatform();
      
      expect(platform).toBeDefined();
      expect(platform.os).toBeOneOf(["Linux", "Darwin", "Windows"]);
      expect(platform.arch).toBeOneOf(["x86_64", "arm64"]);
    });

    it("should generate correct download URL", async () => {
      const { SccService } = await import("../src/impact/scc-service.ts");
      const service = new SccService(join(testHome, "bin", "scc"));
      const platform = { os: "Linux" as const, arch: "x86_64" as const };
      const url = service.getDownloadUrl(platform);
      
      expect(url).toContain("github.com/boyter/scc");
      expect(url).toContain("Linux");
      expect(url).toContain("x86_64");
    });

    it("should initially report scc as not installed", async () => {
      const { SccService } = await import("../src/impact/scc-service.ts");
      const service = new SccService(join(testHome, "bin", "scc"));
      expect(service.isInstalled()).toBe(false);
    });

    it("should detect installed scc binary", async () => {
      const { SccService } = await import("../src/impact/scc-service.ts");
      const sccPath = join(testHome, "bin", "scc");
      const service = new SccService(sccPath);
      
      // Create a mock scc binary
      mkdirSync(join(testHome, "bin"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(sccPath, "#!/bin/bash\necho 'mock scc'");
      chmodSync(sccPath, 0o755);
      
      expect(service.isInstalled()).toBe(true);
    });

    it("should parse scc JSON output correctly", async () => {
      const { SccService } = await import("../src/impact/scc-service.ts");
      const sccPath = join(testHome, "bin", "scc");
      const service = new SccService(sccPath);
      
      // Create a mock scc binary that outputs the correct JSON format
      mkdirSync(join(testHome, "bin"), { recursive: true });
      const mockSccOutput = JSON.stringify([
        {
          "Name": "Python",
          "Bytes": 156,
          "CodeBytes": 0,
          "Lines": 10,
          "Code": 8,
          "Comment": 0,
          "Blank": 2,
          "Complexity": 3,
          "Count": 1,
          "WeightedComplexity": 0,
          "Files": [
            {
              "Language": "Python",
              "PossibleLanguages": ["Python"],
              "Filename": "hello.py",
              "Extension": "py",
              "Location": "hello.py",
              "Symlocation": "",
              "Bytes": 156,
              "Lines": 10,
              "Code": 8,
              "Comment": 0,
              "Blank": 2,
              "Complexity": 3,
              "WeightedComplexity": 0,
              "Hash": null,
              "Binary": false,
              "Minified": false,
              "Generated": false,
              "EndPoint": 0,
              "Uloc": 0
            }
          ],
          "LineLength": null,
          "ULOC": 0
        },
        {
          "Name": "Markdown",
          "Bytes": 100,
          "CodeBytes": 0,
          "Lines": 5,
          "Code": 3,
          "Comment": 0,
          "Blank": 2,
          "Complexity": 0,
          "Count": 1,
          "WeightedComplexity": 0,
          "Files": [
            {
              "Language": "Markdown",
              "PossibleLanguages": ["Markdown"],
              "Filename": "README.md",
              "Extension": "md",
              "Location": "README.md",
              "Symlocation": "",
              "Bytes": 100,
              "Lines": 5,
              "Code": 3,
              "Comment": 0,
              "Blank": 2,
              "Complexity": 0,
              "WeightedComplexity": 0,
              "Hash": null,
              "Binary": false,
              "Minified": false,
              "Generated": false,
              "EndPoint": 0,
              "Uloc": 0
            }
          ],
          "LineLength": null,
          "ULOC": 0
        }
      ]);
      
      // Create mock scc script
      const mockScript = `#!/bin/bash
if [[ "$1" == "--by-file" && "$2" == "-f" && "$3" == "json" ]]; then
  echo '${mockSccOutput}'
else
  echo "Usage: scc --by-file -f json <directory>"
  exit 1
fi`;
      
      writeFileSync(sccPath, mockScript);
      chmodSync(sccPath, 0o755);
      
      // Create a test directory
      const testDir = join(testHome, "test-project");
      mkdirSync(testDir, { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(testDir, "hello.py"), "# Hello\nprint('Hello')\n");
      writeFileSync(join(testDir, "README.md"), "# Test\n\nThis is a test.\n");
      
      // Run scc and parse output
      const metrics = await service.runScc(testDir);
      
      // Verify the parsed metrics
      expect(metrics).toBeDefined();
      expect(metrics.linesOfCode.net).toBe(11); // 8 + 3
      expect(metrics.complexity.cyclomatic).toBe(3);
      expect(metrics.byLanguage).toHaveLength(2);
      expect(metrics.byFile).toHaveLength(2);
      
      // Check language aggregation
      const pythonLang = metrics.byLanguage.find(l => l.language === "Python");
      expect(pythonLang).toBeDefined();
      expect(pythonLang?.code).toBe(8);
      expect(pythonLang?.complexity).toBe(3);
      
      const markdownLang = metrics.byLanguage.find(l => l.language === "Markdown");
      expect(markdownLang).toBeDefined();
      expect(markdownLang?.code).toBe(3);
      expect(markdownLang?.complexity).toBe(0);
      
      // Check file details
      const helloFile = metrics.byFile.find(f => f.path === "hello.py");
      expect(helloFile).toBeDefined();
      expect(helloFile?.code).toBe(8);
      expect(helloFile?.complexity).toBe(3);
      
      const readmeFile = metrics.byFile.find(f => f.path === "README.md");
      expect(readmeFile).toBeDefined();
      expect(readmeFile?.code).toBe(3);
    });

    it("should handle empty scc output", async () => {
      const { SccService } = await import("../src/impact/scc-service.ts");
      const sccPath = join(testHome, "bin", "scc-empty");
      const service = new SccService(sccPath);
      
      // Create a mock scc binary that returns empty array
      mkdirSync(join(testHome, "bin"), { recursive: true });
      const mockScript = `#!/bin/bash
echo '[]'`;
      
      writeFileSync(sccPath, mockScript);
      chmodSync(sccPath, 0o755);
      
      const testDir = join(testHome, "empty-project");
      mkdirSync(testDir, { recursive: true });
      
      const metrics = await service.runScc(testDir);
      
      expect(metrics.linesOfCode.net).toBe(0);
      expect(metrics.complexity.cyclomatic).toBe(0);
      expect(metrics.byLanguage).toHaveLength(0);
      expect(metrics.byFile).toHaveLength(0);
    });
  });
